Sources: FMT — Ex-Penang rep accuses deputy minister of misleading Parliament on Hindu boards

⚠️ Single-source notice: This report is based on a single credible outlet (Free Malaysia Today). No independent corroboration from a second outlet was found at time of publication. The constitutional reference (Item 15(c), Federal List, Ninth Schedule) is independently verifiable.

item.title: Fahmi Says AG-PP Separation Bill Needs 3 More Months — PH's Flagship Reform Keeps Getting Pushed Back
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced that the bill to separate the Attorney General and Public Prosecutor roles requires three more months of refinement before it can be tabled. A select committee chaired by Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will convene on March 16 to examine feedback from MPs. This came just one day after the government failed to pass the PM tenure limit bill — another flagship constitutional reform — falling two votes short of the required two-thirds majority. The AG-PP separation was one of Anwar’s earliest and most prominent reform pledges, first announced as part of his institutional reform package.

🔥 Why It Matters

The AG-PP separation is not a nice-to-have — it is the structural reform most directly connected to prosecutorial independence and the integrity of Malaysia’s legal system. Under the current system, the Attorney General serves as both the government’s chief legal advisor and the nation’s top prosecutor, creating an inherent conflict of interest that has been exploited by every administration. PH campaigned explicitly on fixing this. Three years into power, they are still forming committees and asking for more time. With elections potentially months away, the window for passing constitutional amendments with a supermajority is closing fast — and the MACC-Azam Baki crisis has made the case for an independent prosecutor more urgent than ever.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH has had a parliamentary supermajority since 2022. They have had three years to draft, refine, and pass this bill. Instead, they formed a select committee, which will now meet to “examine feedback” — the bureaucratic equivalent of hitting the snooze button on the alarm you set yourself. The PM tenure bill already failed because the government couldn’t even whip its own MPs to show up for a vote. Now the AG-PP bill needs “three more months” of work. Meanwhile, Attorney General Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar — the man whose dual role this bill is supposed to reform — previously withdrew corruption charges against Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Zahid Hamidi despite a court finding enough evidence to proceed. The conflict of interest isn’t theoretical. It is actively shaping Malaysian justice right now, and PH is asking for another quarter to think about it.

📜 Previous Cases

This is a recurring pattern. The PM tenure limit bill was announced in January 2026 as part of a reform package, tabled in March, and failed on its first attempt. The Freedom of Information Act, also promised, remains in draft. The repeal of the Sedition Act — a signature PH pledge from GE14 — was never delivered during the first PH government and has now been effectively abandoned in favour of new restrictive speech laws. The Peaceful Assembly Act reforms are similarly stalled. PH’s reform agenda reads like a graveyard of good intentions: every pledge is announced with fanfare, delegated to a committee, delayed by “consultations,” and eventually overtaken by the election cycle.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians that Madani governance means institutional reform, not just slogans. You said the AG-PP separation would end the conflict of interest that allowed previous governments to weaponise prosecution. Three years and a supermajority later, your Communications Minister is asking for three more months. Three months will become six. Six will become “after the election.” Malaysians have seen this playbook before — they lived through it under BN for six decades. If you cannot deliver the reforms you promised with the largest parliamentary majority in a generation, you do not deserve another mandate to make the same promises again.

Sources: The Malaysian Reserve | The Star | SCMP

item.title: Negri Sembilan MB Admits His Driver Runs Red Lights — 'I Have No Choice, Look at My Facebook Schedule'
item.description:
Said by: Aminuddin Harun, Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan (PH-Lenggeng)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, 2026, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun publicly admitted that his official car routinely runs red lights, after a viral video showed a vehicle believed to be his using blue strobe lights and a siren to blast through a red light on Jalan Rasah. Speaking to reporters after a state executive council meeting, Aminuddin said: “I admit that we have to beat the light at times, but I have no choice, as there are sometimes several official events lined up in a day.” He pointed to his Facebook page as proof of his hectic schedule. He added that a police officer is always present in the car, and that his office has sought “necessary approvals from the relevant authorities.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not about a traffic violation. This is about a PH state leader publicly declaring that the rules of the road do not apply to him — and framing it as a reasonable accommodation for public service. In a country where ordinary Malaysians receive summonses, demerit points, and fines for the same offence, a Menteri Besar is casually telling reporters he does it regularly and expects understanding. The message to the public is unmistakable: there is one set of rules for leaders and another for everyone else. This is the exact culture of elite entitlement PH promised to dismantle.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Aminuddin’s defence is remarkable in its audacity. He didn’t deny it. He didn’t promise to stop. He told Malaysians to check his Facebook schedule to understand why he needs to break the law. The ‘I had a cop in the car’ justification is not exoneration — it’s an admission that the state apparatus is being used to facilitate rule-breaking by the powerful. If the MB’s schedule is so packed that traffic laws become optional, the answer is better scheduling, not blue strobes and sirens. An apology that ends with ‘but state leaders need some leeway’ is not an apology. It is a declaration of privilege.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of political elites treating traffic laws as suggestions. VIP motorcades, outriders, and sirens have been normalised to the point where ordinary motorists are expected to yield or face consequences. In 2022, a viral video of a minister’s convoy forcing vehicles off the road drew widespread criticism. PH, in opposition, was vocal about such abuses of power. Now in government, their own MB is doing the same thing and asking for sympathy. The entitlement transcends party lines — it’s baked into how Malaysian political elites understand their own importance.

🎯 PH Callout

You campaigned on humility. You told Malaysians that the era of feudal politics and VIP culture was over. Your own Prime Minister once made a show of flying economy class and driving himself. Now your Negri Sembilan MB is running red lights with police cover and telling the rakyat to look at his Facebook to understand why he’s too important to stop at a traffic light. If you can’t even hold your state leaders to basic traffic laws, what hope is there for institutional reform? Follow the rules or change them through proper process. You don’t get to break them and call it public service.

Sources: Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | NST

item.title: [BM] Anwar Guna 'Zionis' Dan 'Bankir' Di Parlimen Untuk Burukkan Balu Daim — Peguam Panggil 'Pembohongan Terang-terangan'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 3 Mac, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Dewan Rakyat bahawa dakwaan komplot untuk menggugat kestabilan kerajaannya — yang dikaitkan dengan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, balu bekas Menteri Kewangan Daim Zainuddin — melibatkan “kumpulan Zionis terkemuka” dan bankir antarabangsa. Beliau mendakwa sebuah firma PR berpusat di UK telah diupah untuk melancarkan serangan terkoordinasi terhadap institusi negara. Ini berlaku sehari selepas MalaysiaNow mendedahkan bahawa pemimpin PKR sendiri pernah mengupah perunding AS pada 2016 untuk membina tekanan Rumah Putih terhadap PM ketika itu Najib Razak. Peguam Na’imah, Rajesh Nagarajan dan Sachpreetraj Singh, mengeluarkan kenyataan pada hari yang sama memanggil dakwaan Anwar “palsu, tidak munasabah, keterlaluan, dan percubaan terang-terangan untuk mengelirukan Parlimen.”

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Anwar menggunakan istilah paling membakar dalam kosa kata politik Malaysia — “Zionis” — di bawah kekebalan parlimen, di mana dia tidak boleh disaman. Ini bukan retorik biasa. Di negara majoriti Islam di mana anti-Zionisme membawa beban emosi yang besar, menggunakan Zionis untuk memburukkan lawan politik adalah penggunaan sentimen awam sebagai senjata secara sengaja. Pemasaan adalah kritikal — Anwar di bawah tekanan atas dakwaan penyalahgunaan SPRM, pendedahan Bloomberg dan desakan yang semakin meningkat dari dalam gabungannya sendiri untuk reformasi sebenar.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Peguam Na’imah menyatakan dengan jelas: “Tiada sepatah perkataan pun tentang Zionisme atau Israel atau apa-apa yang berkaitan dengannya dalam keseluruhan pelan komunikasi.” Dokumen PR itu, kata mereka, adalah tentang meyakinkan bankir tentang keupayaan kewangan keluarga Daim — komunikasi krisis standard untuk keluarga yang menghadapi rampasan aset. Anwar mereka sudut Zionis di Parlimen, di mana dia menikmati kekebalan undang-undang, dengan penuh kesedaran dia tidak akan mengulanginya di luar. Sementara itu, partinya sendiri mengupah perunding asing untuk melobi terhadap PM Malaysia yang memerintah pada 2016. Kemunafikan ini bukan halus — ia struktural. PKR mengupah firma asing adalah strategi politik. Na’imah mengupah firma PR adalah komplot Zionis. Bezanya? Satu melayani kepentingan Anwar. Yang lain mengancamnya.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Anwar mempunyai sejarah panjang menggunakan konspirasi apabila tertekan. Ini adalah taktik yang sama Najib gunakan semasa skandal 1MDB, apabila dia menyalahkan media asing, George Soros dan sabotaj pembangkang atas pendedahan. Anwar menghabiskan sedekad mengejek buku panduan itu. Kini dia menjalankannya.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Kamu berkempen atas ketelusan, akauntabiliti dan reformasi institusi. Kamu beritahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa zaman memburukkan pengkritik sebagai ejen asing sudah berakhir. Kamu ejek Najib kerana menyalahkan Soros. Dan kini, apabila peguam seorang balu menerbitkan dokumen PR yang mengandungi sifar rujukan kepada Zionisme, kamu berdiri di Parlimen — dilindungi oleh kekebalan — dan menggunakan “Zionis” dan “bankir” untuk memburukkannya. Kamu bukan mempertahankan Malaysia daripada campur tangan asing. Kamu menggunakan istilah paling sarat dalam leksikon politik negara untuk menutup penelitian yang sah terhadap kelakuan kerajaan kamu. Itu bukan reformasi. Itu politik era Najib dengan pelekat reformasi padanya.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow | Reuters | Malay Mail

item.title: Anwar Invokes 'Zionists' and 'Bankers' in Parliament to Smear Daim's Widow — Lawyers Call It an 'Outright Lie'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told the Dewan Rakyat that the alleged plot to destabilise his government — attributed to Na’imah Abdul Khalid, the widow of former Finance Minister Daim Zainuddin — involved “prominent Zionist groups” and international bankers. He claimed a UK-based PR firm had been engaged to launch a coordinated attack on national institutions. This came one day after MalaysiaNow revealed that PKR leaders, including figures close to Anwar, had themselves engaged a US consultant in 2016 to build White House pressure on then-PM Najib Razak. Na’imah’s lawyers, Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh, issued a statement the same day calling Anwar’s claims “false, preposterous, outrageous, and a blatant attempt to mislead Parliament.”

🔥 Why It Matters

Anwar used the most incendiary term in Malaysian political vocabulary — “Zionist” — under parliamentary immunity, where he cannot be sued. This is not casual rhetoric. In a Muslim-majority country where anti-Zionism carries enormous emotional weight, invoking Zionists to discredit a political opponent is a deliberate weaponisation of public sentiment. It is the same playbook PH once accused UMNO and PAS of running: using fear and identity to distract from institutional accountability. The timing is critical — Anwar is under pressure over the MACC abuse allegations, Bloomberg exposés, and growing calls from within his own coalition for real reform. The “Zionist plot” narrative reframes domestic accountability demands as foreign sabotage.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Na’imah’s lawyers stated unequivocally: “There is not one word about Zionism or Israel or anything related to it in the entire communications plan.” The PR document, they said, was about reassuring bankers of the Daim family’s financial viability — standard crisis communications for a family facing asset seizure. Anwar fabricated the Zionist angle in Parliament, where he enjoys legal immunity, knowing full well he would not repeat it outside. Meanwhile, his own party engaged foreign consultants to lobby against a sitting Malaysian prime minister in 2016. The hypocrisy is not subtle — it is structural. PKR engaging foreign firms to pressure Putrajaya was political strategy. Na’imah engaging a PR firm to protect her family’s banking relationships is a Zionist plot. The difference? One serves Anwar’s interests. The other threatens them.

📜 Previous Cases

Anwar has a long history of invoking conspiracy when cornered. During the MACC crisis in February-March 2026, he accused critics of being part of a plot by “wealthy elites” manipulating media. He dismissed Bersih’s call for MACC accountability as “unfair.” He told the public not to act as “prosecutor or judge” during the KK Mart firebombing crisis. The “foreign plot” narrative is a classic authoritarian deflection — and it is the exact tactic Najib used during the 1MDB scandal, when he blamed foreign media, George Soros, and opposition sabotage for the exposés. Anwar spent a decade mocking that playbook. Now he is running it.

🎯 PH Callout

You campaigned on transparency, accountability, and institutional reform. You told Malaysians that the days of smearing critics as foreign agents were over. You mocked Najib for blaming Soros. You mocked Mahathir for blaming “foreign speculators.” And now, when a widow’s lawyers publish a PR document that contains zero references to Zionism, you stand in Parliament — protected by immunity — and invoke “Zionists” and “bankers” to discredit her. You are not defending Malaysia from foreign interference. You are using the most loaded term in the country’s political lexicon to shut down legitimate scrutiny of your government’s conduct. That is not reform. That is not even competent spin. That is Najib-era politics with a reformasi sticker on it.

Sources: MalaysiaNow | Reuters | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kutuk Serangan AS-Israel Ke Atas Iran Di Parlimen — 'Kita Tak Boleh Telan Lagi' Ceramah Hak Asasi Barat, AP PH Serukan Boikot Kedutaan AS
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 2 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim membentangkan usul tergempar di Dewan Rakyat mengutuk serangan AS dan Israel ke atas Iran, yang bermula pada 28 Februari dan membunuh sekurang-kurangnya 201 orang termasuk Pemimpin Tertinggi Iran Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar memanggil serangan itu “pelanggaran terang-terangan undang-undang antarabangsa” dan menyifatkan ceramah Barat tentang hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi sebagai kemunafikan:

“Dalam pergaulan saya dengan kepimpinan Amerika dan Eropah, saya tidak selesa dengan ceramah mereka tentang hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi. Kita tidak boleh menelan hujah-hujah sedemikian daripada mereka lagi.”

Usul itu diluluskan sebulat suara dengan sokongan kedua-dua pihak. Tetapi saat paling penting secara politik datang daripada ahli parlimen belakang PH Syahredzan Johan (Bangi), yang menyeru semua AP memboikot acara anjuran kedutaan AS di Kuala Lumpur.

Anwar juga memberi isyarat penempatan semula diplomatik ke arah Iran, menyeru hubungan pendidikan dan perdagangan yang lebih rapat.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Kutukan itu sendiri tidak luar biasa — Malaysia secara konsisten menyokong kedaulatan Palestin dan Iran. Apa yang penting adalah peningkatan retorik dan jurang antara kata-kata dan tindakan.

Pertama, Anwar memanggil ceramah hak asasi Barat “kemunafikan” adalah kaya datang daripada perdana menteri yang kerajaannya sendiri di bawah serangan kerana: melindungi ketua SPRM Azam Baki, menggunakan siasatan polis untuk menyasarkan pengkritik, menggugurkan separuh hukuman Najib sementara hutang 1MDB masih berbilion.

Kedua, seruan Syahredzan untuk memboikot kedutaan AS adalah populisme pamer dengan akibat diplomatik sebenar. Ekonomi Malaysia bergantung kepada perdagangan dan pelaburan AS. AS adalah rakan dagang ketiga terbesar Malaysia.

Ketiga, beralih ke arah Iran adalah tidak bertanggungjawab dari segi diplomatik. Mazhab Syiah Iran secara rasmi dianggap “menyimpang” di Malaysia yang majoriti Sunni. Anwar memberi isyarat solidariti dengan negara teokratik yang identiti agamanya dijenayahkan oleh kerajaannya sendiri.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Mari kira percanggahan:

Soal kemunafikan hak asasi: Anwar mengatakan dia “tidak boleh menelan lagi” ceramah hak asasi Barat. Ini orang yang sama yang menghabiskan bertahun-tahun melobi kerajaan Barat, NGO dan media untuk hak asasinya sendiri — semasa dipenjarakan di bawah Mahathir dan Najib. Kini penglibatan asing adalah “komplot Zionis” dan piawai hak asasi Barat adalah “kemunafikan.” Satu-satunya yang berubah ialah sebelah mana meja PM dia duduk.

Soal boikot kedutaan AS: Syahredzan Johan menyeru boikot diplomatik sementara kerajaannya sendiri mengekalkan hubungan diplomatik, perdagangan dan ketenteraan penuh dengan AS. Malaysia baru menandatangani Perkongsian Strategik Komprehensif dengan Washington pada Oktober 2025.

Soal solidariti Iran: Malaysia menjenayahkan amalan Syiah. Anwar menyatakan solidariti dengan Iran sambil menganiaya penganut semazhab di tanah air — ini bukan berprinsip, ia oportunistik.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Anwar, kamu beritahu Parlimen: “Kita tidak boleh menelan hujah-hujah mereka lagi.”

Adil. Tetapi rakyat juga tidak boleh menelan hujah kamu lagi.

Kamu ceramah Barat tentang kemunafikan sementara ketua SPRM kamu duduk atas RM800,000 saham yang tidak didedahkan. Kamu panggil advokasi asing “komplot Zionis” selepas menghabiskan berdekad melakukan perkara yang sama. Ini bukan dasar luar yang berprinsip. Ini populisme musim Ramadan.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] PKR Upah Perunding AS Untuk Lobi Washington Terhadap Najib — Kini Menjerit Salah Bila Orang Lain Buat Sama
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada Mac 2026, MalaysiaNow menerbitkan emel bocor yang mendedahkan PKR telah mengupah sebuah firma perundingan politik berpusat di Washington pada 2016 untuk melobi Amerika Syarikat menekan Perdana Menteri ketika itu Najib Razak. Emel tersebut termasuk dokumen 84 muka surat yang membuat tuduhan serius terhadap Najib, dengan matlamat mendapatkan Washington untuk secara terbuka mengecam kerajaan Malaysia.

Bekas naib presiden PKR Sivarasa Rasiah mengesahkan komunikasi tersebut, mengatakan pemimpin parti termasuk beliau sendiri terlibat. Beliau merangkanya sebagai advokasi yang sah untuk membebaskan Anwar Ibrahim, yang ketika itu seorang tahanan politik.

Pendedahan ini datang ketika kerajaan Anwar secara serentak menuduh keluarga bekas menteri kewangan Daim Zainuddin menggunakan ejen asing untuk menggugat kestabilan pentadbiran semasa — memanggilnya ancaman keselamatan negara.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Ini mendedahkan piawai berganda yang besar di teras strategi politik PH semasa. Anwar merangka dakwaan lobi asing keluarga Daim sebagai pengkhianatan — komplot untuk menjatuhkan kerajaan menggunakan pengaruh asing. Tetapi partinya sendiri melakukan perkara yang sama apabila mereka dalam pembangkang.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Pembelaan Sivarasa — “kami cuba membebaskan tahanan politik” — mudah tetapi tidak tahan penelitian. Dokumen 84 muka surat itu bukan sahaja meminta pembebasan Anwar. Ia membuat tuduhan menyeluruh terhadap Najib dan secara jelas meminta campur tangan AS dalam politik domestik Malaysia. Itu lobi asing. Noktah.

Apabila PKR melakukannya, ia “advokasi.” Apabila keluarga Daim didakwa melakukannya, ia “konspirasi Zionis” dan ancaman keselamatan negara. Piawai berganda begitu terang-terangan sehingga pemerhati biasa pun boleh nampak. Anwar pada dasarnya mengatakan: lobi asing adalah patriotik apabila kami melakukannya, dan pengkhianatan apabila lawan kami melakukannya. Itu bukan prinsip — ia kemudahan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Parti politik Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang lobi antarabangsa. UMNO mengekalkan pelobi Washington selama berdekad. Anwar sendiri memupuk hubungan mendalam dengan pembuat dasar AS, badan pemikir dan media semasa tahun-tahun pembangkangan. Kemarahan terpilih mengikuti corak yang sama seperti layanan PH terhadap kebebasan akhbar (Bloomberg bagus apabila mendedahkan Najib, Bloomberg buruk apabila mendedahkan Azam Baki).

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Kamu mengupah firma AS untuk melobi Washington terhadap PM Malaysia yang memerintah, menghasilkan dokumen 84 muka surat tuduhan, dan meminta campur tangan Amerika dalam politik Malaysia. Bekas naib presiden kamu sendiri mengesahkannya. Kini kamu beritahu rakyat Malaysia apabila orang lain melakukan perkara yang sama, ia konspirasi Zionis untuk menjatuhkan kerajaan? Pilih satu laluan. Sama ada lobi asing adalah bahagian sah advokasi politik — maka berhenti menjenayahkan lawan kamu — atau ia ancaman keselamatan negara, maka pemimpin PKR sendiri perlu disiasat juga. Kamu tak boleh ambil kedua-duanya.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow | MalaysiaNow - Dokumen | Malaysiakini

item.title: Anwar Condemns US-Israel Strikes on Iran in Parliament — 'We Can No Longer Swallow' Western Human Rights Lectures, PH MP Calls for US Embassy Boycott
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 2 March 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim tabled an emergency motion in the Dewan Rakyat condemning the US and Israel’s strikes on Iran, which began on 28 February and killed at least 201 people including Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar called the strikes a “flagrant violation of international law” and described Western lectures on human rights and democracy as hypocrisy:

“In my engagements with the American and European leadership, I am uneasy about their lectures on human rights and democracy. We can no longer swallow such arguments from them.”

The motion passed unanimously with bipartisan support. Opposition Leader Hamzah Zainudin praised Anwar for a “stern and firm statement.”

But the most politically significant moment came from PH backbencher Syahredzan Johan (Bangi), who called on all MPs to boycott events hosted by the US embassy in Kuala Lumpur:

“Let’s not go. Even though we get invited to their Independence Day celebrations, let’s not go. We need to show that we can no longer accept what they do.”

Anwar also signaled a diplomatic repositioning toward Iran, calling for closer education and trade ties, claiming Malaysia was “the only country to show a firm, brave stance.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

The condemnation itself is unremarkable — Malaysia has consistently supported Palestinian and Iranian sovereignty. What matters is the escalation in rhetoric and the gap between words and actions.

First, Anwar calling Western human rights lectures “hypocrisy” is rich coming from a prime minister whose own government is under fire for:

  • Protecting MACC chief Azam Baki despite multiple corruption allegations
  • Using police investigations to target critics under the guise of a “Zionist plot”
  • Halving Najib’s sentence while the 1MDB debt still runs into billions
  • Failing to pass even basic institutional reforms like the PM tenure bill Anwar is lecturing the West about hypocrisy while running a government that has abandoned virtually every reform promise it made.

Second, Syahredzan’s call to boycott the US embassy is performative populism with real diplomatic consequences. Malaysia’s economy depends on US trade and investment. The US is Malaysia’s third-largest trading partner. Calling for a diplomatic boycott from the floor of Parliament is not a principled stand — it’s cheap political theatre that someone else will have to clean up.

Third, the pivot toward Iran is diplomatically reckless. Iran’s Shia denomination is officially considered “deviant” in Sunni-majority Malaysia. Anwar is signaling solidarity with a theocratic state whose religious identity his own government criminalises. The contradiction is absurd.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Let’s count the contradictions:

On human rights hypocrisy:

  • Anwar says he can “no longer swallow” Western lectures on human rights. This is the same man who spent years lobbying Western governments, NGOs, and media outlets for his own human rights — during his imprisonment under Mahathir and Najib. His entire political comeback was built on international sympathy and foreign advocacy.

  • Now that he’s in power, foreign advocacy is a “Zionist plot” and Western human rights standards are “hypocrisy.” The only thing that changed is which side of the PM’s desk he sits on. On the US embassy boycott:

  • Syahredzan Johan is a PH backbencher calling for a diplomatic boycott while his own government maintains full diplomatic, trade, and military relations with the US. Malaysia just signed a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership with Washington in October 2025.

  • If PH is serious about opposing US actions, why stop at embassy cocktail parties? Cancel the trade deal. Recall the ambassador. Oh wait — that would cost money. On Iran solidarity:

  • Malaysia criminalises Shia practice. Anwar’s own religious affairs apparatus treats Shia Muslims as deviants. Declaring solidarity with Iran while persecuting its co-religionists at home is not principled — it’s opportunistic.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

  • 2018-2022: Anwar’s own foreign lobbying. Anwar and PKR allies engaged Washington consultancies, international media, and foreign governments to pressure the Najib and Muhyiddin administrations. He accepted this support gratefully. Now foreign engagement is subversion.
  • October 2025: Malaysia-US Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Just five months before calling US human rights lectures “hypocrisy,” Anwar’s government signed an upgraded bilateral deal with Washington covering trade, critical minerals, and market access.
  • Malaysia’s track record on Iran. Malaysia has historically been cautious with Iran due to the Sunni-Shia divide. Anwar’s pivot is a departure from decades of careful diplomatic hedging, driven by the emotional politics of the moment rather than strategic calculation.
  • 2024-2025: Gaza solidarity without substance. Malaysia has been vocal on Palestine but has taken no meaningful economic or diplomatic action against Israel beyond rhetoric. The pattern is clear: loud words, zero follow-through.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

Anwar, you told Parliament: “We can no longer swallow such arguments from them.”

Fair enough. But the rakyat can no longer swallow yours either.

You lecture the West on hypocrisy while your MACC chief sits on RM800,000 in undisclosed shares. You call foreign advocacy a “Zionist plot” after spending decades doing exactly the same thing. You signal solidarity with Iran while your religious authorities criminalise Shia practice at home.

And your backbencher calls for boycotting US embassy parties — the absolute lowest-cost, lowest-risk form of protest imaginable — while your government maintains a full strategic partnership with Washington.

This isn’t principled foreign policy. It’s Ramadan-season populism. You’re performing outrage for domestic consumption while doing nothing that would actually cost you anything.

If you want to be taken seriously on human rights and sovereignty, start at home. Fix your own institutions. Reform your own enforcement agencies. Stop using conspiracy theories to silence critics. Then — and only then — will anyone take your lectures to the West seriously.

Sources: Straits Times | Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | FocusMalaysia

item.title: PKR Hired US Consultants to Lobby Washington Against Najib — Now Cries Foul When Others Do the Same
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In March 2026, MalaysiaNow published leaked emails revealing that PKR had engaged a Washington-based political consultancy firm in 2016 to lobby the United States to exert pressure on then-Prime Minister Najib Razak. The emails included an 84-page dossier making serious allegations against Najib, with the goal of getting Washington to publicly rebuke the Malaysian government.

Former PKR vice-president Sivarasa Rasiah confirmed the communications, saying party leaders including himself were involved. He framed it as legitimate advocacy to free Anwar Ibrahim, who was then a political prisoner.

The revelation came as Anwar’s government was simultaneously accusing former finance minister Daim Zainuddin’s family of using foreign agents to destabilise the current administration — calling it a national security threat.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This exposes a massive double standard at the heart of PH’s current political strategy. Anwar is framing Daim’s family’s alleged foreign lobbying as treasonous — a plot to topple the government using foreign influence. But his own party did the exact same thing when they were in opposition.

Opposition MP Shahidan Kassim pressed Anwar in Parliament to explain the difference. The answer matters because the government is using the Daim case to justify expanded national security rhetoric, deflect from the Azam Baki scandal, and paint critics as foreign agents. If PKR’s own lobbying was legitimate, then the same standard must apply to others.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Sivarasa’s defence — “we were trying to free a political prisoner” — is convenient but doesn’t hold up under scrutiny. The 84-page dossier didn’t just ask for Anwar’s release. It made sweeping allegations against Najib and explicitly sought US intervention in Malaysian domestic politics. That’s foreign lobbying. Period.

When PKR does it, it’s “advocacy.” When Daim’s family allegedly does it, it’s a “Zionist conspiracy” and a national security threat. The double standard is so blatant that even a casual observer can see through it.

Anwar’s government is essentially saying: foreign lobbying is patriotic when we do it, and treasonous when our opponents do it. That’s not a principle — it’s a convenience.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysian political parties have a long history of international lobbying. UMNO maintained Washington lobbyists for decades. Anwar himself cultivated deep relationships with US policymakers, think tanks, and media during his years in opposition. None of this was considered problematic when PH was the beneficiary.

The selective outrage follows the same pattern as PH’s treatment of press freedom (Bloomberg good when exposing Najib, Bloomberg bad when exposing Azam Baki) and institutional accountability (MACC is weaponised under BN, MACC is independent under PH).

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You hired a US firm to lobby Washington against a sitting Malaysian PM, produced an 84-page dossier of allegations, and sought American intervention in Malaysian politics. Your own former vice-president confirmed it. Now you’re telling Malaysians that when others do the same thing, it’s a Zionist-backed conspiracy to topple the government? Pick a lane. Either foreign lobbying is a legitimate part of political advocacy — in which case stop criminalising your opponents for it — or it’s a national security threat, in which case PKR’s own leaders should be investigated too. You can’t have it both ways.

Sources: MalaysiaNow | MalaysiaNow - Dossier | The Star | Malaysiakini | Malaysiakini - Sivarasa

item.title: [BM] Anwar Menjalankan Buku Panduan Mahathir 2.0 — Orang Berbeza, Naluri Autokratik Sama
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pelbagai analisis bebas daripada ISEAS, CSIS, Institut Keamanan Toda dan East Asia Forum berkumpul pada kesimpulan yang sama: gaya pemerintahan Anwar Ibrahim semakin mencerminkan gaya Mahathir Mohamad — orang yang memenjarakannya. Pemusatan kuasa (PM+MK), penggunaan undang-undang represif, tindakan keras terhadap media, pendakwaan terpilih, politik berasaskan kaum, pameran dasar luar sebagai gangguan domestik dan pengurusan gabungan melalui naungan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Anwar menghabiskan kehidupan politiknya sebagai ANTI-Mahathir. Seluruh naratifnya adalah: saya menderita di bawah sistem autokratik Mahathir, saya faham kejahatannya, dan saya akan membina sesuatu yang berbeza. Konvergensi antara gaya pemerintahan Anwar dan Mahathir bukan sekadar ironi — ia bukti muktamad bahawa budaya politik Malaysia, bukannya pemimpin individu, menghasilkan pemerintahan autokratik. Menukar wajah di puncak tidak mengubah sistem.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Undang-undang represif? Mahathir ada ISA, Anwar ada SOSMA dan Akta Hasutan. Kawalan media? Mahathir ada PPPA, Anwar ada sekatan SKMM dan kod etika wartawan. Naungan? Mahathir ada kroni, Anwar ada 47.5 pelantikan GLC/tahun. Politik kaum? Mahathir ada DEB, Anwar ada dasar Bumiputera ’tidak boleh dirunding’. Pemusatan kuasa? Mahathir pegang PM+MK, Anwar pegang PM+MK. Pameran dasar luar? Mahathir ada ’nilai Asia,’ Anwar ada ucapan Palestin. Senarai semak itu serupa.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Demokrasi Malaysia mempunyai corak: pemimpin pembangkang menjanjikan reformasi, memenangi kuasa, kemudian mentadbir seperti pendahulu mereka. Mahathir pada 1981 menjanjikan reformasi, kemudian membina sistem yang Anwar lawan. Anwar pada 2022 berjanji merobohkan sistem itu, kemudian menerimanya secara borong. Analisis CSIS menamakannya dengan sempurna: ‘Kematian Reformasi.’ Bukan kerana gerakan itu gagal dari luar — tetapi kerana pemimpinnya membunuhnya dari dalam.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu adalah Mahathir 2.0 dengan bahasa Inggeris yang lebih baik dan kisah latar yang lebih bersimpati. Dasarnya sama. Struktur kuasanya sama. Naungannya sama. Autoritarianismenya sama. Satu-satunya bezanya ialah kamu TAHU ia salah kerana kamu menghabiskan 11 tahun dalam penjara kerananya. Dan kamu pilih untuk melakukannya juga. Itu bukan sekadar kemunafikan — itu pengkhianatan terhadap semua orang yang percaya kamu berbeza.

Sumber: CSIS — Kematian Reformasi | Institut Keamanan Toda — Laporan 184 | East Asia Forum — Aspirasi Reformasi

item.title: Anwar Is Running Mahathir's Playbook 2.0 — Different Man, Same Authoritarian Instincts
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple independent analyses from ISEAS, CSIS, Toda Peace Institute, and East Asia Forum converge on the same conclusion: Anwar Ibrahim’s governance style increasingly mirrors that of Mahathir Mohamad — the man who imprisoned him. Centralisation of power (PM+FM), use of repressive laws, crackdown on media, selective prosecution, race-based politics, foreign policy grandstanding as domestic distraction, and coalition management through patronage.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Anwar spent his political life as the ANTI-Mahathir. His entire narrative was: I suffered under Mahathir’s authoritarian system, I understand its evils, and I will build something different. The convergence between Anwar’s governing style and Mahathir’s is not just ironic — it’s the final proof that Malaysian political culture, rather than individual leaders, produces authoritarian governance. Changing the face at the top doesn’t change the system.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Repressive laws? Mahathir had ISA, Anwar has SOSMA and the Sedition Act. Media control? Mahathir had PPPA, Anwar has MCMC blocks and journalist codes of ethics. Patronage? Mahathir had cronies, Anwar has 47.5 GLC appointees/year. Race politics? Mahathir had NEP, Anwar has ’non-negotiable’ Bumiputera policies. Concentration of power? Mahathir held PM+FM, Anwar holds PM+FM. Foreign policy preening? Mahathir had ‘Asian values,’ Anwar has Palestine speeches. The checklist is identical.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysian democracy has a pattern: opposition leaders promise reform, win power, then govern like their predecessors. Mahathir in 1981 promised reform, then built the system Anwar fought against. Anwar in 2022 promised to dismantle that system, then adopted it wholesale. The CSIS analysis titled it perfectly: ‘The Death of Reformasi.’ Not because the movement failed from outside — but because its leader killed it from within.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You are Mahathir 2.0 with better English and a more sympathetic backstory. The policies are the same. The power structures are the same. The patronage is the same. The authoritarianism is the same. The only difference is you KNOW it’s wrong because you spent 11 years in prison because of it. And you chose to do it anyway. That’s not just hypocrisy — it’s a betrayal of everyone who believed you were different.

Sources: CSIS — The Death of Reformasi | Toda Peace Institute — Report 184 | East Asia Forum — Reform Aspirations

item.title: [BM] Saman Fitnah RM100J Azam Baki Terhadap Bloomberg — Ketua SPRM Lawan Media Bukan Rasuah
item.description:
Said by: Bloomberg / Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-27

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 27 Februari 2026, dilaporkan bahawa Ketua Pesuruhjaya SPRM Azam Baki memfailkan saman fitnah RM100 juta terhadap Bloomberg. Saman itu datang selepas Bloomberg menerbitkan siasatan yang mendakwa Azam memiliki berjuta-juta saham dalam syarikat tersenarai awam — berpotensi melanggar had pemilikan penjawat awam. Bloomberg seterusnya mendakwa pegawai SPRM telah berpakat dengan ahli perniagaan untuk merancang pengambilalihan bermusuhan firma tersenarai, menggunakan serbuan dan ugutan untuk memaksa eksekutif menjual saham kepada kumpulan yang digelar “mafia korporat.” Respons Anwar ialah pasukan petugas — bukan siasatan bebas, bukan Suruhanjaya Diraja. DAP sendiri kata pasukan petugas “tidak cukup jauh.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Ketua agensi antirasuah Malaysia sedang menyaman organisasi berita kewangan paling terkemuka di dunia kerana melaporkan dakwaan salah laku kewangannya. Ini bukan kes rakyat swasta mempertahankan reputasi — ini ketua penguatkuasa undang-undang antirasuah menggunakan sistem perundangan untuk menggeruni organisasi media yang menyiasatnya. Angka RM100J bukan tentang pampasan; ia tentang pencegahan. Agensi antirasuah melawan kewartawanan rasuah dan bukannya rasuah sebenar adalah puncak ironi Madani.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Respons Anwar — pasukan petugas — adalah setara birokrasi dengan doa dan harapan. Pasukan petugas melapor kepada kerajaan. Siasatan bebas melapor kepada orang ramai. Anwar memilih mekanisme yang mengekalkan kawalan di tangannya, bukan yang memaksimumkan ketelusan. CNA melaporkan bahawa Anwar menghadapi “krisis integriti” dengan “perpecahan parti PKR” dan “kegelisahan gabungan PH.” Orang yang membina Reformasi atas janji tadbir urus bersih kini mengetuai kerajaan di mana ketua antirasuah menyaman wartawan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Skandal pemilikan saham Azam Baki pertama kali muncul pada awal 2022, apabila Malaysiakini melaporkan dia memegang saham dalam dua syarikat tersenarai melebihi had pemilikan perkhidmatan awam. Jawatankuasa parlimen khas membersihkannya — mencetuskan kemarahan meluas. Siasatan Bloomberg 2025-2026 melangkah lebih jauh, mendakwa pakatan sulit sistemik antara pegawai SPRM dan perompak korporat.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu menjanjikan kebebasan SPRM. Sebaliknya, ketua SPRM menyaman Bloomberg kerana melakukan kerja SPRM. Siasatan bebas — bukan pasukan petugas — adalah respons minimum yang boleh diterima. Jika Azam tidak bersalah, siasatan membersihkannya. Jika dia bersalah, infrastruktur antirasuah negara perlu dibina semula dari asas. Pasukan petugas kamu adalah mekanisme penangguhan, bukan mekanisme akauntabiliti.

Sumber: CNA | SCMP

item.title: Azam Baki's RM100M Defamation Suit Against Bloomberg — MACC Chief Fights Media Instead of Corruption
item.description:
Said by: Bloomberg / Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 27 February 2026, it was reported that MACC Chief Commissioner Azam Baki filed a RM100 million defamation suit against Bloomberg. The suit came after Bloomberg published an investigation alleging that Azam owned millions of shares in publicly listed companies — potentially breaching civil servant ownership limits. Bloomberg further alleged that MACC officials had colluded with businessmen to orchestrate hostile takeovers of listed firms, using raids and intimidation to force executives into selling shares to groups dubbed the “corporate mafia.” Rather than resigning or submitting to an independent investigation, Azam chose to sue the messenger. Anwar’s response was to announce a task force — not an independent inquiry, not a Royal Commission. Even DAP, his own coalition partner, said a task force “does not go far enough.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The head of Malaysia’s anti-corruption agency is suing the world’s most prominent financial news outlet for reporting on his alleged financial misconduct. This is not a case of a private citizen defending their reputation — this is the chief enforcer of anti-corruption law using the legal system to intimidate a media organisation that investigated him. If Azam’s role is to fight corruption, and a credible international outlet alleges he is compromised, the correct institutional response is a transparent, independent investigation — not a defamation suit designed to chill future reporting. The RM100M figure is not about compensation; it is about deterrence. Bloomberg has stood by its reporting. The anti-corruption agency fighting corruption journalism instead of actual corruption is peak Madani irony.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s response — a task force — is the bureaucratic equivalent of thoughts and prayers. A task force reports to the government. An independent inquiry reports to the public. Anwar chose the one that protects him. DAP publicly said a task force “does not go far enough,” meaning even Anwar’s own coalition partners don’t trust his anti-corruption mechanisms. CNA reported that Anwar faces a “graft crisis” with “PKR party fractures” and “PH coalition jitters.” The man who built Reformasi on the promise of clean governance now leads a government where the anti-corruption chief sues journalists, the PM’s aide gets charged with bribery, and the DPM’s 47 charges get dropped. At some point, the pattern is the policy.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Azam Baki’s share ownership scandal first surfaced in early 2022, when Malaysiakini reported he held shares in two public-listed companies exceeding the civil service ownership cap. A special parliamentary committee cleared him — prompting widespread outrage. Bloomberg’s 2025-2026 investigation went further, alleging systemic collusion between MACC officials and corporate raiders. The MACC-media adversarial pattern is not new either: the commission has previously threatened legal action against outlets that reported on its internal affairs. Under Azam’s leadership, MACC has become less an anti-corruption body and more a political instrument — selectively prosecuting opposition figures while shielding coalition allies.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised MACC independence. Instead, the MACC chief is suing Bloomberg for doing MACC’s job. An independent inquiry — not a task force — is the minimum acceptable response. If Azam is innocent, an inquiry clears him. If he is not, the country’s anti-corruption infrastructure needs to be rebuilt from scratch. Your task force is a stalling mechanism, not an accountability mechanism. Even your own coalition partner sees through it. Do better, or stop pretending Reformasi means anything.

Sources: CNA | SCMP

item.title: [BM] Dakwaan 'Mafia Korporat' SPRM — DAP Desak Siasatan Bebas, Anwar Enggan
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leaders / SCMP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir Februari 2026, pemimpin DAP secara terbuka menyatakan bahawa pasukan petugas yang dicadangkan Anwar untuk memeriksa dakwaan salah laku di SPRM “tidak cukup jauh.” Mereka mendesak siasatan bebas. SCMP melaporkan desakan itu datang selepas siasatan Bloomberg mendakwa pegawai SPRM berpakat dengan ahli perniagaan untuk merancang pengambilalihan bermusuhan firma tersenarai — dakwaan “mafia korporat.” Respons Anwar — pasukan petugas terkawal kerajaan dan bukannya suruhanjaya bebas — meletakkan “agenda reformasinya di bawah penelitian baharu.” Malah rakan gabungannya sendiri tidak mempercayai mekanisme antirasuahnya.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Apabila rakan gabungan kamu sendiri — parti yang paling dikaitkan dengan reformasi tadbir urus — secara terbuka mengatakan respons antirasuah kamu tidak mencukupi, jurang kredibiliti tidak lagi boleh dinafikan. DAP bukan pembangkang. DAP adalah sekutu Anwar. Jika DAP tidak mempercayai pasukan petugas untuk menyampaikan akauntabiliti, kenapa orang lain patut percaya? Perbezaan antara pasukan petugas dan siasatan bebas bukan semantik — ia struktural. Pasukan petugas melapor kepada PM. Siasatan bebas melapor kepada orang ramai. Anwar memilih mekanisme yang mengekalkan kawalan di tangannya.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Seluruh jenama politik Anwar dibina atas memerangi rasuah. Bloomberg mendakwa ketua antirasuahnya terjejas. Rakan gabungannya sendiri kata responsnya terlalu lemah. Dan jawapannya… pasukan petugas. Ini orang yang menghabiskan bertahun-tahun di penjara atas pertuduhan rasuah bermotif politik. Dia tahu betul apa yang berlaku apabila institusi antirasuah ditawan oleh politik. Namun apabila berhadapan dengan dakwaan sahih penawanan SPRM, dia mencapai alat yang paling lemah. Pasukan petugas itu tidak direka untuk mencari kebenaran — ia direka untuk menguruskan kitaran berita sehingga ceritanya mati.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Ini mengikuti corak. Apabila Shamsul Iskandar ditangkap mengeluarkan surat sokongan, respons Anwar adalah “teguran.” Apabila 47 pertuduhan Zahid digugurkan, responsnya senyap. Apabila skandal perlombongan Sabah Farhash Wafa tercetus, SPRM membersihkannya sementara Bloomberg mendakwa Anwar mengarahkan SPRM tidak menyiasat. Setiap kali dakwaan rasuah menyentuh lingkaran dalaman Anwar, responsnya sentiasa pilihan paling lemah yang ada.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Rakan gabungan kamu sendiri memberitahu kamu secara terbuka bahawa respons kamu tidak mencukupi. Dengarlah mereka. Tubuhkan siasatan bebas dengan kuasa sepina, pendengaran awam dan mandat untuk menyiasat SPRM dari atas ke bawah. Jika kamu tak mahu dengar DAP, dengar prinsip yang pernah memenjarakanmu: bahawa institusi antirasuah mesti bebas daripada kawalan politik. Pasukan petugas yang dikawal PM menyiasat ketua antirasuah PM bukan kebebasan — ia sandiwara.

Sumber: SCMP

item.title: [BM] Setiap Ahli Kabinet Capai KPI, Kata Anwar — Pengkritik Tanya: KPI Apa?
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 25 Februari 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Parlimen bahawa setiap ahli kabinet memenuhi KPI mereka dan menunjukkan prestasi berintegriti. Dia tidak mendedahkan apa KPI tersebut, bagaimana ia diukur atau siapa yang menilainya. Anwar menjawab ahli parlimen pembangkang yang bertanya sama ada menteri akan dipaksa meletak jawatan kerana prestasi lemah. Jawapannya: “Akauntabiliti bukan sahaja tentang hukuman tetapi juga pembetulan, penambahbaikan berterusan, dan tanggungjawab moral yang dipegang menteri untuk rakyat.” Tiada KPI pernah didedahkan secara awam untuk mana-mana menteri dalam kabinet Anwar. Tiada menteri pernah dikenakan hukuman, diturunkan pangkat atau ditegur secara awam kerana prestasi lemah.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Mendakwa pematuhan KPI 100% tanpa mendedahkan KPI adalah setara korporat memberi diri sendiri ulasan prestasi yang sempurna dan enggan tunjukkan borangnya. Ia bukan akauntabiliti — ia pensijilan sendiri. Jika setiap menteri memenuhi KPI mereka, maka sama ada KPI begitu rendah sehingga kegagalan mustahil, atau penilaian itu tidak jujur. Memandangkan 77% rakyat Malaysia melaporkan bergelut dengan kos sara hidup, kedudukan universiti merosot dan janji reformasi kekal tidak dipenuhi, dakwaan itu menggelikan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: PM yang memegang kedua-dua portfolio PM dan Menteri Kewangan pada dasarnya menilai dirinya sendiri atas pengurusan fiskal. Tiada semakan bebas terhadap pejabat paling berkuasa di negara ini. Singapura menerbitkan metrik prestasi menteri dan mengaitkannya dengan sasaran penyampaian khidmat awam. Korea Selatan menjalankan penilaian prestasi kerajaan tahunan dengan keputusan yang diterbitkan. Pendekatan Malaysia di bawah PM “reformis”: percaya katanya. Tinjauan Pusat Merdeka Februari 2026 menunjukkan kepuasan orang ramai terhadap pengurusan ekonomi pada 38% — mencadangkan bahawa apa sahaja KPI yang wujud, orang ramai tidak bersetuju ia dipenuhi.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap PM Malaysia pernah mendakwa kabinetnya berprestasi baik. Najib mendakwa menterinya menyampaikan keputusan — sementara 1MDB berdarah berbilion. Muhyiddin mendakwa pengurusan pandemik berkesan — sementara ribuan mati. Coraknya sejagat: penilaian sendiri tanpa pengesahan luaran sentiasa menghasilkan skor sempurna.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Terbitkan KPI. Teliti setiap menteri, setiap metrik, setiap sasaran dan setiap keputusan. Letakkan dalam talian. Biar rakyat lihat sama ada KPI Menteri Pendidikan termasuk skor PISA yang merosot, sama ada KPI Menteri Kesihatan termasuk 33,000 doktor kontrak masih menunggu jawatan tetap, sama ada KPI Menteri Ekonomi termasuk 77% rakyat Malaysia bergelut dengan kos. Kamu takkan — kerana kad skor itu akan menghancurkan. Kerajaan yang menilai diri sendiri secara rahsia tiada kredibiliti mendakwa akauntabiliti.

Sumber: FMT

item.title: [BM] SPRM Dalam Krisis Tanpa Kepimpinan Boleh Dipercayai — Badan Pemerhati Antirasuah Kini Menjadi Skandal
item.description:
Said by: DAP / Bloomberg / CNA Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Suruhanjaya Pencegahan Rasuah Malaysia (SPRM) dilanda krisis kredibiliti paling teruk dalam sejarahnya. Ketua Pesuruhjaya Azam Baki berdepan dakwaan Bloomberg memiliki berjuta-juta ringgit saham (melanggar had pegawai awam) dan bersubahat dengan ‘mafia korporat.’ Bukannya meletak jawatan, Azam memfailkan saman fitnah RM100 juta terhadap Bloomberg. DAP, rakan gabungan PH sendiri, mendesak siasatan bebas, kata pasukan petugas kerajaan ’tidak cukup jauh.’ CNA menyifatkannya sebahagian daripada ‘ribut sempurna’ Anwar.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Jika agensi antirasuah sendiri korup, keseluruhan kerangka tadbir urus runtuh. SPRM sepatutnya menjadi institusi yang mempertanggungjawabkan semua pihak lain. Apabila SPRM sendiri menjadi subjek dakwaan rasuah — dan tindak balas kerajaan hanya pasukan petugas yang tidak berkesan berbanding siasatan bebas — ia mengisyaratkan tiada institusi di Malaysia yang boleh dipercayai.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Janji tadbir urus utama Anwar ialah mengukuhkan institusi antirasuah. Beliau mencadangkan memisahkan peranan AG dan Pendakwa Raya (tergendala). Beliau menjanjikan kebebasan SPRM (ketua agensi itu sendiri kini menjadi skandal). Beliau menjanjikan toleransi sifar terhadap rasuah (pembantu sendiri didakwa rasuah). Setiap janji antirasuah bukan sahaja dimungkiri — ia diterbalikkan. Infrastruktur antirasuah lebih teruk di bawah PH, bukan lebih baik.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah BN, SPRM dilihat ramai sebagai terjejas secara politik, digunakan menyasarkan ahli politik pembangkang sambil mengabaikan rasuah parti pemerintah. Di bawah PH 1.0, SPRM pernah memiliki kredibiliti singkat ketika menyiasat 1MDB. Di bawah PH 2.0, kredibiliti SPRM jatuh ke paras paling rendah — ketua berperang dengan Bloomberg di mahkamah sementara agensinya sendiri berdepan dakwaan menjalankan rangkaian pemerasan korporat. Perjalanannya: terjejas → seketika dipercayai → lebih teruk dari sebelumnya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Rakan gabungan anda sendiri kata tindak balas anda terhadap krisis SPRM tidak mencukupi. Bloomberg — salah satu saluran berita kewangan paling dihormati dunia — berpegang pada laporannya mengenai Azam Baki. Suruhanjaya antirasuah anda menjalankan saman RM100 juta terhadap kewartawanan berbanding melawan rasuah. Dan tindak balas anda? Pasukan petugas. Untuk dakwaan rasuah paling serius dalam sejarah SPRM. Itu bukan reformasi — itu mengurus optik sementara institusi terbakar.

Sumber: SCMP — Tekanan Meningkat Terhadap Anwar Soal SPRM | CNA — Anwar Berdepan Krisis Rasuah

item.title: Every Cabinet Member Meeting KPIs, Says Anwar — Critics Ask: What KPIs?
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 25 February 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told Parliament that every cabinet member is meeting their KPIs and performing with integrity. He did not disclose what those KPIs were, how they were measured, or who evaluated them. Anwar was responding to an opposition MP who asked if ministers would be forced to resign for poor performance. His reply: “Accountability is not only about punishment but also correction, continuous improvement, and the moral responsibility ministers held for the people.” No KPIs have ever been publicly disclosed for any minister in Anwar’s cabinet. No minister has been penalised, demoted, or publicly reprimanded for underperformance. Two ministers were removed in the December 2025 reshuffle — including Rafizi Ramli — but Anwar framed it as “portfolio realignment,” not performance accountability.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Claiming 100% KPI compliance without disclosing the KPIs is the corporate equivalent of giving yourself a perfect performance review and refusing to show the form. It is not accountability — it is self-certification. If every minister is meeting their KPIs, then either the KPIs are so low that failure is impossible, or the assessment is dishonest. Given that 77% of Malaysians report struggling with cost of living, university rankings are dropping, the CPI is stuck at 1MDB-era levels, and reform promises remain unmet, the claim is laughable on its face. Transparency means publishing the KPIs, the methodology, and the results — and letting the public judge. “Trust me, everyone is great” is not reform governance.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: A PM who holds both the PM and Finance Minister portfolios is essentially grading himself on fiscal management. There is no independent check on the most powerful office in the country. The Najib government also claimed ministers were performing. The Mahathir government also claimed results were being delivered. Without published metrics, every government is equally excellent — which means the exercise is meaningless. Singapore publishes ministerial performance metrics and links them to public service delivery targets. South Korea conducts annual government performance evaluations with published results. Malaysia’s approach under the “reformist” PM: trust his word. The Merdeka Center’s February 2026 poll showed public satisfaction with economic management at 38% — suggesting that whatever KPIs exist, the public disagrees they are being met.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian PM has claimed their cabinet is performing well. Najib claimed his ministers delivered results — while 1MDB bled billions. Muhyiddin claimed pandemic management was effective — while thousands died and billions in stimulus were unaccounted for. The pattern is universal: self-assessment without external verification always produces perfect scores. Anwar promised to be different. He isn’t. The KPI charade is a bipartisan tradition that PH has enthusiastically adopted.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Publish the KPIs. Go through every minister, every metric, every target, and every result. Put it online. Let the rakyat see whether their Education Minister’s KPIs include declining PISA scores, whether the Health Minister’s KPIs include 33,000 contract doctors still waiting for permanent posts, whether the Economy Minister’s KPIs include 77% of Malaysians struggling with costs. You won’t — because the scorecard would be devastating. A government that grades itself in secret has no credibility claiming accountability.

Sources: FMT

item.title: MACC 'Corporate Mafia' Allegations — DAP Demands Independent Inquiry, Anwar Resists
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leaders / SCMP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late February 2026, DAP leaders publicly stated that Anwar’s proposed task force to examine alleged misconduct at MACC “does not go far enough.” They demanded an independent inquiry instead. SCMP reported that the push came after Bloomberg’s investigation alleged MACC officials had colluded with businessmen to orchestrate hostile takeovers of listed firms — the so-called “corporate mafia” allegations. Anwar’s response — a government-controlled task force rather than an independent commission — put his “reform agenda under renewed scrutiny.” Even his own coalition allies don’t trust his anti-corruption mechanisms.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When your own coalition partner — the party most associated with governance reform — publicly says your anti-corruption response is inadequate, the credibility gap is no longer deniable. DAP is not the opposition. DAP is Anwar’s ally. If DAP doesn’t trust the task force to deliver accountability, why should anyone else? The distinction between a task force and an independent inquiry is not semantic — it is structural. A task force reports to the PM. An independent inquiry reports to the public. Anwar chose the mechanism that keeps control in his hands, not the one that maximises transparency.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s entire political brand is built on fighting corruption. Bloomberg alleges his anti-corruption chief is compromised. His own coalition partner says his response is too weak. And his answer is… a task force. This is a man who spent years in prison over politically motivated corruption charges. He knows exactly what happens when anti-corruption institutions are captured by politics. Yet when faced with credible allegations of MACC capture, he reaches for the weakest possible tool. The task force is not designed to find the truth — it is designed to manage the news cycle until the story dies.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This follows a pattern. When Shamsul Iskandar was caught issuing support letters, Anwar’s response was a “reprimand.” When Zahid’s 47 charges were dropped, the response was silence. When Farhash Wafa’s Sabah mining scandal broke, MACC cleared him while Bloomberg alleged Anwar ordered MACC not to investigate. Every time corruption allegations touch Anwar’s inner circle, the response is always the weakest available option — never an independent investigation, never structural reform, never real consequences.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own coalition partner is telling you publicly that your response is inadequate. Listen to them. Establish an independent inquiry with subpoena powers, public hearings, and a mandate to investigate MACC from top to bottom. If you won’t listen to DAP, listen to the principle you once went to prison for: that anti-corruption institutions must be independent of political control. A task force controlled by the PM investigating the PM’s anti-corruption chief is not independence — it is theatre.

Sources: SCMP

item.title: MACC in Crisis with No Credible Leadership — The Anti-Corruption Watchdog Has Become the Scandal
item.description:
Said by: DAP / Bloomberg / CNA Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is embroiled in its worst-ever credibility crisis. Chief Commissioner Azam Baki faces Bloomberg allegations of owning millions in shares (breaching civil servant limits) and colluding with a ‘corporate mafia.’ Rather than resign, Azam filed a RM100 million defamation suit against Bloomberg. DAP, PH’s own coalition partner, has demanded an independent inquiry, saying the government’s task force ‘does not go far enough.’ CNA called it part of Anwar’s ‘perfect storm.’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: If the anti-corruption agency itself is corrupt, the entire governance framework collapses. MACC is supposed to be the institution that holds everyone else accountable. When MACC is the subject of corruption allegations — and the government’s response is a toothless task force rather than an independent inquiry — it signals that no institution in Malaysia is trustworthy.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s signature governance promise was strengthening anti-corruption institutions. He proposed separating the AG and Public Prosecutor roles (stalled). He promised MACC independence (the agency’s own chief is now the scandal). He promised zero tolerance for corruption (his own aide was charged with bribery). Every anti-corruption promise has not just been broken — it’s been inverted. The anti-corruption infrastructure is worse under PH, not better.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, MACC was widely perceived as politically compromised, used to target opposition politicians while ignoring ruling party corruption. Under PH 1.0, MACC briefly had credibility when it investigated 1MDB. Under PH 2.0, MACC’s credibility has hit rock bottom — the chief is fighting Bloomberg in court while his own agency faces allegations of running a corporate extortion ring. The trajectory: compromised → briefly credible → worse than ever.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own coalition partner says your response to the MACC crisis is inadequate. Bloomberg — one of the world’s most respected financial news outlets — is standing by its reporting against Azam Baki. Your anti-corruption commission is running a RM100 million lawsuit against journalism instead of fighting corruption. And your response is a ’task force.’ A task force. For the most serious corruption allegation in MACC’s history. That’s not reform — that’s managing the optics while the institution burns.

Sources: SCMP — Pressure Mounts on Anwar Over MACC | CNA — Anwar Faces Graft Crisis

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Dakwa Anwar Dalang Serangan Media Sosial Terhadapnya — Perpecahan Jadi Terbuka
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-22

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 22 Februari 2026, bekas Menteri Ekonomi Rafizi Ramli secara terbuka mendakwa Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim dalang kempen media sosial yang diselaraskan menyasarkannya. Business Today melaporkan Rafizi menuduh Anwar “menyemarakkan pengaruh media sosialnya” — menggunakan akaun yang diselaraskan untuk menyerang kredibilitinya dan menjejaskan kedudukan politiknya dalam PKR. SCMP membuat susulan pada 18 Februari, merangka Rafizi sebagai merampas “panji reformasi dan kerajaan bersih yang dahulunya milik Anwar sendiri” dan bertanya sama ada beliau kini “nurani gerakan Reformasi.” Anwar menolak perpecahan sebagai “gangguan.” Rafizi sebelum ini memberi amaran kepada Anwar bahawa CPI yang mendatar di 50 mata “amat berbahaya” bagi kredibiliti kerajaan. Ganjarannya: disingkirkan dari kabinet dalam rombakan Disember 2025. Portfolio Kementerian Ekonomi dibiarkan kosong selama enam bulan selepas pemergiannya.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Apabila bekas menteri anda yang paling cekap menuduh anda menjalankan operasi pengaruh terhadapnya, jenama reformasi sedang merosot teruk. Ini bukan suara pembangkang — ini bekas Menteri Ekonomi, ahli pengasas PKR, berkata PM menggunakan helah kotor terhadap rakyatnya sendiri. Jika dakwaan itu benar, pejabat PM mengalihkan sumber kepada peperangan politik dalaman berbanding mentadbir. Jika ia palsu, perpecahan masih nyata dan parti retak secara terbuka menjelang pilihan raya seterusnya. Sama ada cara, demokrasi dalaman PKR runtuh — parti yang dibina atas cabaran kepimpinan autoritarian kini dipimpin seorang yang didakwa menghukum perbezaan pendapat dengan serangan media sosial yang diselaraskan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Rafizi bukan pembangkang. Beliau PKR. Beliau parti Anwar sendiri. Jika PM menggunakan media sosial untuk mendisiplinkan pengkritik dalaman, itu tingkah laku ketua parti autoritarian, bukan pemimpin demokratik. Ia jenis perkara yang Najib pernah dituduh — dan yang Anwar sendiri pernah kutuk. Rangkaian SCMP mengenai Rafizi sebagai “nurani Reformasi” patut menggerunkan Anwar — ia bermakna media antarabangsa perasan gelaran itu tidak lagi sesuai untuk PM. Apabila anak didik anda mewarisi jenama anda kerana anda meninggalkannya, keputusannya jelas. Orang yang berkata benar tentang risiko rasuah dalam kerajaan dihukum kerananya. Itu bukan reformasi — itu pembalasan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Dakwaan Rafizi mengenai serangan media sosial yang diselaraskan menggema corak dalam kerajaan lain yang cenderung autoritarian di mana pemimpin menggunakan pasukan siber untuk mendisiplinkan perbezaan pendapat dalaman. Dalam PKR sendiri, Rafizi sebelum ini bercanggah dengan pertubuhan parti mengenai pemilihan calon dan dasar ekonomi. Amarannya tentang skandal Farhash Wafa diabaikan; Farhash kemudian didakwa rasuah. Amarannya tentang skor CPI ditolak; skor kekal di paras era 1MDB. Coraknya konsisten: Rafizi menimbulkan kebimbangan yang sah, kepimpinan mengabaikan atau menghukumnya, dan kebimbangan itu kemudian terbukti wajar.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Menteri yang memberi amaran kepada anda tentang rasuah dalam pejabat anda sendiri betul. Menteri yang memberitahu anda skor CPI “amat berbahaya” betul. Tindak balas anda ialah menyingkirkannya dan didakwa menjalankan kempen media sosial terhadapnya. Jika anda mahu membuktikan Rafizi salah, terbitkan perakaunan penuh perbelanjaan media sosial dan hubungan kontraktor PMO. Jika anda tidak boleh, dakwaan itu berdiri — dan nurani gerakan Reformasi kini milik orang yang anda buang, bukan orang di Putrajaya.

Sumber: Business Today | SCMP

item.title: Rafizi Alleges Anwar Is Fueling Social Media Attacks Against Him — Rift Goes Public
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 22 February 2026, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli publicly alleged that Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim was behind coordinated social media campaigns targeting him. Business Today reported Rafizi accused Anwar of “fueling his social media influence” — using coordinated accounts to attack his credibility and undermine his political standing within PKR. SCMP followed up on 18 February, framing Rafizi as seizing “the banner of reform and clean government that was once Anwar’s own” and asking whether he was now “the conscience of the Reformasi movement.” Anwar dismissed the rift as a “distraction.” Rafizi had previously warned Anwar that the CPI flatline at 50 points was “extremely dangerous” for the government’s credibility. His reward: being sidelined from cabinet in the December 2025 reshuffle. The Economy Ministry portfolio sat vacant for six months after his departure.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When your most competent former minister accuses you of running influence operations against him, the reform brand is in terminal decline. This is not opposition noise — this is the former Economy Minister, a PKR founding member, saying the PM is using dirty tricks against his own people. If the allegations are true, the PM’s office is diverting resources to internal political warfare instead of governing. If they are false, the rift is still real and the party is fracturing publicly ahead of the next election. Either way, PKR’s internal democracy is collapsing — the party built on challenging authoritarian leadership is now led by a man who allegedly punishes dissent with coordinated social media attacks.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Rafizi is not opposition. He is PKR. He is Anwar’s own party. If the PM is using social media to discipline internal critics, that is the behaviour of an authoritarian party boss, not a democratic leader. It is the kind of thing Najib was accused of — and which Anwar once condemned. The SCMP framing Rafizi as “the conscience of Reformasi” should alarm Anwar deeply — it means international media has noticed the title no longer fits the PM. When your protégé inherits your brand because you abandoned it, the verdict is clear. The man who told the truth about corruption risks within the government was punished for it. That is not reform — that is retaliation.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Rafizi’s allegations of coordinated social media attacks echo patterns in other authoritarian-leaning governments where leaders use cyber troops to discipline internal dissent. Within PKR itself, Rafizi had previously clashed with the party establishment over candidate selection and economic policy. His warning about the Farhash Wafa scandal was ignored; Farhash was later charged with bribery. His warning about the CPI score was dismissed; the score remains at 1MDB-era levels. The pattern is consistent: Rafizi raises legitimate concerns, the leadership ignores or punishes him, and the concerns later prove justified.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The minister who warned you about corruption in your own office was right. The minister who told you the CPI score was “extremely dangerous” was right. Your response was to sideline him and allegedly run social media campaigns against him. If you want to prove Rafizi wrong, publish a full accounting of the PMO’s social media spending and contractor relationships. If you can’t do that, the allegation stands — and the “Reformasi” movement’s conscience now belongs to the man you discarded, not the man in Putrajaya.

Sources: Business Today | SCMP

item.title: [BM] Kongres Khas DAP 12 Julai — Parti Soal Peranan Sendiri Dalam Kerajaan Sambil Kekal Dalam Kerajaan
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-21

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 21 Februari 2026, KM Pulau Pinang Chow Kon Yeow mengumumkan DAP akan mengadakan kongres khas pada 12 Julai 2026 untuk memutuskan peranan parti dalam kerajaan Anwar. Chow menegaskan DAP ’tidak menarik sokongan daripada kerajaan Madani’ tetapi membincangkan ‘sama ada terus memegang jawatan menteri dan jawatan tertentu.’ 40 AP DAP akan terus menyokong kerajaan. Semua ahli, termasuk pemimpin, mesti mematuhi keputusan kongres.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: DAP mengancam meninggalkan kerajaan tanpa benar-benar meninggalkan kerajaan. Kongres khas adalah teater politik — cara menekan Anwar untuk terma yang lebih baik sambil mengekalkan keselamatan keahlian gabungan. Jika DAP benar-benar mempunyai perbezaan dasar, mereka akan menyatakannya secara terbuka. Sebaliknya, mereka mengadakan undian dalaman mengenai ‘sama ada memegang jawatan menteri’ — yang merupakan taktik rundingan berselindung di sebalik perbincangan demokratik.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Ini ibarat mengancam berhenti kerja sambil terus mengambil gaji. DAP mahukan kredit atas kebebasan (‘kami mempersoal peranan kami!’) sambil menikmati manfaat kuasa (‘kami tidak benar-benar keluar!’). Kongres khas memberikan DAP kuasa rundingan untuk rombakan kabinet seterusnya tanpa sebarang risiko sebenar. Jika kongres mengundi untuk kekal — yang memang akan berlaku — DAP boleh berkata ‘ahli yang memutuskan.’ Jika mereka mengundi untuk meninggalkan kabinet — yang tidak akan berlaku — ia akan meruntuhkan kerajaan. Kedua-dua hasil menguntungkan DAP; tiada yang menguntungkan rakyat.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: DAP mempunyai sejarah drama dalaman mengenai peranannya dalam kerajaan gabungan. Pada era Mahathir 2.0, DAP berdepan ketegangan serupa mengenai kompromi dengan PPBM. Corak konsisten: DAP memasuki gabungan, berkompromi pada prinsip, berdepan bantahan dalaman, mengadakan acara dalaman dramatik, kemudian kekal. Kitaran berulang kerana alternatif — pembangkang — lebih buruk untuk jentera parti.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Jika anda mempunyai perbezaan dasar dengan kerajaan, nyatakan secara terbuka. Jika anda rasa gabungan tidak memberikan untuk pengundi anda, nyatakan kenapa. Tetapi jangan adakan kongres khas untuk ‘memutuskan peranan anda’ sambil serentak berjanji terus menyokong kerajaan. Itu bukan perbincangan demokratik — itu taktik rundingan. Rakyat boleh melihat menembuisinya.

Sumber: Malay Mail | Bernama

item.title: DAP Special Congress July 12 — Party Questions Its Own Role in Government While Staying in Government
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 21 February 2026, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow announced that DAP would hold a special congress on 12 July 2026 to decide the party’s role in Anwar’s government. Chow insisted DAP was ’not withdrawing support from the Madani government’ but was discussing ‘whether to continue holding ministerial posts and certain positions.’ DAP’s 40 MPs would continue supporting the government. All members, including leaders, must abide by the congress decision.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: DAP is threatening to leave government without actually leaving government. The special congress is political theatre — a way to pressure Anwar for better terms while maintaining the safety of coalition membership. If DAP genuinely had policy disagreements, they’d articulate them publicly. Instead, they’re holding an internal vote on ‘whether to hold ministerial posts’ — which is a negotiation tactic dressed up as democratic deliberation.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: This is the political equivalent of threatening to quit your job while continuing to cash the paycheck. DAP wants credit for independence (‘we’re questioning our role!’) while enjoying the benefits of power (‘we’re not actually leaving!’). The special congress gives DAP negotiating leverage for the next cabinet reshuffle without any real risk. If the congress votes to stay — which it will — DAP gets to say ’the members decided.’ If they vote to leave cabinet — which they won’t — it would collapse the government. Both outcomes benefit DAP; neither benefits the rakyat.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: DAP has a history of internal drama over its role in coalition governments. In the Mahathir 2.0 era, DAP faced similar tensions over compromises with PPBM. The pattern is consistent: DAP enters coalitions, compromises on principles, faces internal backlash, holds dramatic internal events, then stays. The cycle repeats because the alternative — opposition — is worse for the party apparatus.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If you have policy disagreements with the government, state them publicly. If you think the coalition isn’t delivering for your voters, say why. But don’t hold a special congress to ‘decide your role’ while simultaneously promising to keep supporting the government. That’s not democratic deliberation — it’s a negotiating ploy. The rakyat can see through it.

Sources: Malay Mail | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Gabungan Perpaduan 'Goyah' — Mandat Reformasi Tidak Ditunaikan, Perebutan Kuasa Terdedah
item.description:
Said by: UCA News Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: ‘Kerajaan Perpaduan Malaysia semakin kelihatan bukan seperti gabungan besar yang dibentuk atas kepentingan nasional tetapi lebih seperti gencatan senjata rapuh antara parti yang tidak lagi mempercayai satu sama lain.’ PH berkempen atas ikrar yang jelas, khusus, boleh diukur secara awam — kebanyakannya masih belum ditunaikan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Apabila pemerhati antarabangsa yang bersimpati pun menggambarkan gabungan anda sebagai ‘goyah,’ naratif telah beranjak daripada ‘bolehkah mereka menyampaikan?’ kepada ‘berapa lama mereka boleh bertahan?’ Kerajaan perpaduan dijual sebagai kompromi perlu untuk menghalang puak pelampau. Tetapi kompromi tanpa penyampaian hanyalah perkongsian kuasa untuk kepentingan sendiri.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Ikrar manifesto yang tidak ditunaikan — pemansuhan AUKU, penghapusan tol, penguatkuasaan undang-undang antilompat parti, reformasi institusi — semakin bertimbun. Setiap janji yang dimungkiri menghakis perbezaan moral antara PH dan kerajaan yang digantikannya. Gabungan yang wujud hanya untuk menghalang alternatif daripada mentadbir bukan mentadbir — ia menduduki.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: PH 1.0 di bawah Mahathir juga menjanjikan reformasi menyeluruh dan hampir tidak menyampaikan apa-apa sebelum runtuh dalam Langkah Sheraton. Corak sama: bentuk gabungan, janji reformasi, terperangkap dalam perebutan kuasa dalaman, sampaikan apa-apa pun tidak, dan harap ketakutan terhadap alternatif mengekalkan kesetiaan pengundi. Pengundi Malaysia kini menyaksikan dua kerajaan PH gagal menunaikan janji yang sama. Pada satu ketika, alasan itu habis.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda beritahu pengundi kerajaan perpaduan perlu untuk kestabilan dan reformasi. Anda dapat kestabilan (hampir-hampir). Mana reformasi? Setiap bulan yang berlalu tanpa menunaikan manifesto sendiri, anda kelihatan kurang seperti kerajaan reformasi dan lebih seperti pengaturan perkongsian kuasa tanpa tujuan selain pemeliharaan diri.

Sumber: UCA News — Gabungan Perpaduan Goyah

item.title: Unity Coalition 'Faltering' — Reform Mandate Unmet, Power Struggles Naked
item.description:
Said by: UCA News Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: ‘Malaysia’s Unity Government is beginning to look less like a grand coalition forged in the national interest and more like a fragile ceasefire among parties that no longer trust one another.’ PH campaigned on clear, specific, publicly measurable pledges — most remain unmet.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When even sympathetic international observers describe your coalition as ‘faltering,’ the narrative has shifted from ‘can they deliver?’ to ‘how long can they survive?’ The unity government was sold as a necessary compromise to keep extremists out. But compromise without delivery is just power-sharing for its own sake.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Unmet manifesto pledges — UUCA repeal, toll abolition, anti-hopping law enforcement, institutional reform — are piling up. Each broken promise erodes the moral distinction between PH and the governments it replaced. A coalition that exists only to prevent the alternative from governing is not governing — it is occupying.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: PH 1.0 under Mahathir also promised sweeping reform and delivered almost nothing before collapsing in the Sheraton Move. The pattern is identical: form a coalition, promise reform, get bogged down in internal power struggles, deliver nothing, and hope the fear of the alternative keeps voters loyal.
