Every day you govern with UMNO holding veto power over reform is another day Reformasi dies — not with a bang, but with a coalition meeting.

Sources: UCA News | CNA

item.title: Najib House Arrest Bid Rejected — Court Rules Royal Addendum Invalid
item.description:
Said by: High Court Judge Alice Loke / Najib Razak

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 22 December 2025, the KL High Court ruled that the royal addendum allowing convicted former PM Najib Razak to serve his remaining 1MDB sentence under house arrest was invalid. Judge Alice Loke said the King’s discretion must be exercised “for the public good” and found the addendum lacked proper legal basis. Al Jazeera and Reuters both reported the ruling. The Selangor Sultan had earlier warned that pardons should not be “arbitrary for certain parties.” Anwar’s government had already halved Najib’s sentence via the Pardons Board in February 2024 — the house arrest bid was the next step in an escalating pattern of leniency for a convicted kleptocrat whose party Anwar now depends on.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The house arrest addendum was widely seen as a backroom deal to keep UMNO in the coalition. Najib’s sentence was first halved (from 12 to 6 years), then the house arrest addendum emerged as a way to get him out of prison entirely while technically still “serving” his sentence. The court’s rejection exposed the political machinery behind it — this was not clemency based on merit, but leniency engineered for coalition management. The judiciary held the line that the executive would not. Judge Alice Loke’s ruling was a rare moment of institutional independence in a system where every other check has failed.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar built his political career opposing Najib. He spent years calling 1MDB the greatest financial scandal in Malaysian history. He demanded accountability. Then he took power, needed UMNO’s votes, and began systematically reducing the consequences for the man who stole billions. First, the Pardons Board halved the sentence. Then, the house arrest addendum appeared. If the court hadn’t intervened, Najib — convicted of stealing from the Malaysian people — would be living in a mansion while the country still owes RM50 billion from his scandal. The man who caused the debt gets a mansion. The rakyat who pays the debt gets toll hikes and diesel price increases. This is Anwar’s Malaysia Madani.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a troubled history with pardons and sentence reductions for politically connected convicts. Anwar himself benefited from a royal pardon after GE14 — which his supporters argued was justified given the politically motivated nature of his conviction. The difference with Najib is that his conviction was for an actual financial crime with billions in documented losses. Halving his sentence and attempting house arrest sets a precedent: if you steal enough and your party is useful enough, the consequences are negotiable. The Selangor Sultan’s warning — that pardons should not be “arbitrary for certain parties” — was a rare royal rebuke that went unheeded by the government.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The judiciary saved you from yourself. If the court hadn’t ruled the addendum invalid, you would have put a convicted kleptocrat in a mansion while Malaysians pay RM50 billion for his crimes. You already halved his sentence. You already dropped his DPM’s 47 charges. How much more leniency does UMNO need before you admit that Reformasi is dead and coalition survival is all that matters? The court held the line. Will you?

Sources: Al Jazeera | Reuters

item.title: Anwar Says 'Long Way to Go' on Elections in December 2025 — Now Preparing Snap GE16 for 2026
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 17, 2025, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told editors and senior journalists at a briefing in Seri Perdana that he had ruled out any possibility of snap general elections in 2026. “The election still has a long way to go. I have not thought about it,” he said. “I am focusing on how we continue to implement reforms and alleviate the problems people face regarding the higher cost of living.”

By February 2026, less than two months later, speculation was mounting that GE16 could be called as early as July 2026. CNA reported that party insiders and civil servants confirmed preparations were underway. Reuters reported in March 2026 that coalition partners were already positioning themselves for snap polls.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a PM lying to the press about his election timeline. In December, he said elections were “a long way to go” and he hadn’t even thought about it. By February, his own party machinery was gearing up for a snap election. The current mandate doesn’t expire until February 2028 — calling elections nearly two years early is a major strategic decision that doesn’t happen overnight.

Anwar either lied to the press in December, or he made a radical strategic shift in under two months. Neither explanation inspires confidence. If he lied, it’s calculated deception of the public. If he genuinely hadn’t considered it, then the snap election push was driven by panic over the Azam Baki scandal, the PKR internal fractures, and coalition instability — not strategic planning.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

“I have not thought about it.” That’s what the PM said in December. The same PM who is the chairman of his party, the head of his coalition, and a career politician who has been thinking about elections every day for 30 years.

Of course he was thinking about it. The Sabah election in November 2025 — where PH was routed — had already forced a strategic reckoning. The PKR party election mess, Rafizi’s departure, and the cabinet reshuffle were all moves that only make sense if you’re positioning for an early general election.

Anwar told journalists what they wanted to hear — “focus on reforms, focus on cost of living” — while his political operation was already calculating seat projections and coalition arithmetic. This is not a man who hasn’t thought about elections. This is a man who doesn’t want you to know he’s thinking about elections.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Every Malaysian PM who has called snap elections has denied planning them right up until the announcement. Najib denied planning GE14 until he dissolved Parliament. Muhyiddin denied planning the Melaka and Johor state elections until they were called. It’s a Malaysian political tradition: lie about election timing, then call elections when it’s strategically advantageous.

Anwar is doing exactly what he criticised his predecessors for doing. The reformist is following the BN playbook.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You told the press in December that elections were “a long way to go.” Two months later, your coalition is gearing up for snap polls. Either you lied in December or you’re making major democratic decisions on the fly. Neither is acceptable for a PM who claims to be different from his predecessors. If you’re going to call an early election, at least be honest about why — don’t pretend it’s about reform when it’s about survival.

Sources: The Star | Straits Times | CNA | Reuters | NST

item.title: Hannah Yeoh Appointment Sparks Race-Based Backlash — PAS and UMNO Both Object
item.description:
Said by: Takiyuddin Hassan, PAS Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2025, Anwar appointed Hannah Yeoh as Federal Territories Minister in his cabinet reshuffle. PAS Secretary-General Takiyuddin Hassan immediately objected — not to Yeoh’s qualifications, but to her race. He argued the appointment would “strengthen DAP’s influence” in urban areas and disadvantage Malays. Rightist Malay groups warned that DAP was now “controlling Malay landholding” through the Federal Territories portfolio, which oversees KL, Putrajaya, and Labuan. Even UMNO leaders quietly raised concerns. Anwar hit back: “To reject a person because of her colour and race, this is atrocious in this day and age.” Social media reactions were polarised.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The backlash exposes how normalised ethno-religious gatekeeping remains in Malaysian politics — even under a supposedly reformist government. A minister was judged not by competence, track record, or policy vision, but by ethnicity. This is the structural racism PH was elected to dismantle. Instead, the party that appointed Yeoh simultaneously downgraded other DAP portfolios in the same reshuffle to signal “Malay strength” to voters. Yeoh’s appointment was brave — but it was also the exception that proved the rule. The rest of the reshuffle was racial calculus.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The contradiction is structural: Anwar wants to appear multiracial while managing a coalition that requires Malay-first optics. He appoints one Chinese-Malaysian woman to a prominent portfolio, then compensates by downgrading DAP elsewhere and elevating UMNO ministers. The multiracial gesture is real but it is hedged — designed to be just bold enough for DAP voters, but not so bold that Malay voters feel threatened. Analyst Azmi Hassan said the reshuffle “can only be read as sending a signal to Malays that Anwar is pushing for stronger Malay presence in government.” Yeoh’s appointment is the progressive leaf on an ethno-nationalist tree.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every DAP appointment to a senior portfolio triggers the same race-based backlash. When Lim Guan Eng became Finance Minister in PH 1.0, Malay groups protested. When DAP won state seats, opponents framed it as Chinese encroachment. The cycle is depressingly predictable: appoint a non-Malay, face racial backlash, either stand firm (briefly) or retreat. Anwar stood firm on Yeoh — which is commendable — but his overall cabinet strategy still operates within the racial framework rather than dismantling it. A government that makes one multiracial appointment while conducting the rest of its governance on racial lines is not reforming race relations — it is managing optics.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Defending Hannah Yeoh’s appointment was the right thing to do. Now defend the principle behind it — that competence matters more than ethnicity — across your ENTIRE government. Stop making one multiracial appointment while conducting racial calculus everywhere else. If you believe race shouldn’t determine cabinet positions, prove it with every appointment — not just the one that gets you international praise. And if PAS objects because a minister is Chinese, call it what it is: racism. Not “cultural concern,” not “community sensitivity” — racism. You said it was “atrocious.” Now act like you mean it.

Sources: Malay Mail | Focus Malaysia

item.title: Anwar's Cabinet Reshuffle Sparks Race Controversy — Hannah Yeoh as FT Minister, Adam Adli as Religious Affairs Deputy
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 16, 2025, Anwar Ibrahim announced his second cabinet reshuffle, involving 28 ministerial and deputy ministerial changes. Among the most controversial appointments:

Hannah Yeoh was named Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) — the first non-Malay to hold the portfolio. PAS and right-wing Malay groups immediately attacked the appointment, claiming it would strengthen “DAP dominance” in KL and threaten Malay interests. Anwar called the criticism “atrocious” and said race should not determine ministerial capability.

Adam Adli — a former student activist — was appointed Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), sparking questions about whether he had the credentials or background to oversee Islamic governance matters.

The reshuffle came after months of vacancies following Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s resignations, and was widely seen as positioning for a potential snap election.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

The Hannah Yeoh appointment exposed the fundamental contradiction of Malaysian politics: a government that claims to be multiracial but operates in a system where race determines who can hold which portfolio. The FT Minister portfolio had always been held by a senior UMNO Malay. Anwar’s decision to give it to a Chinese DAP leader was either a bold reform move or a political miscalculation that handed ammunition to the opposition.

The Adam Adli appointment raised legitimate questions about competence. Religious affairs governance requires specific knowledge of Islamic jurisprudence, state-federal religious dynamics, and the politics of religious authorities. Appointing a former student activist with no demonstrated expertise in these areas looked like a political reward, not a merit-based decision.

The Straits Times noted that “controversies cloud Malaysian PM Anwar’s new Cabinet line-up” — not the kind of headline you want when you’re positioning for a snap election.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

There’s bullshit on multiple sides here. The race-based attacks on Hannah Yeoh are deplorable — she has a strong track record across multiple portfolios. Her race should be irrelevant to her competence.

But Anwar walked into this controversy with his eyes open. He knew appointing a non-Malay FT minister would trigger a racial backlash. If he wanted to make that stand on principle, fine — but then commit to it. Don’t make the appointment and then have UMNO’s Zahid Hamidi rush out to reassure Malays that it won’t undermine the “Malay agenda.” That sends the message that the appointment needs defending on racial grounds, which undermines the very principle Anwar claimed to be championing.

And the Adam Adli appointment? That’s just patronage. Reward a loyal activist with a portfolio he’s not qualified for. It’s exactly what PH criticised BN for doing.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Every Malaysian cabinet reshuffle generates racial controversy — it’s baked into the system. But PH was supposed to transcend this. In 2018, the appointment of Lim Guan Eng as Finance Minister was attacked on racial grounds. PH defended it then on merit. Seven years later, the same party is still fighting the same battles — suggesting that PH’s “multiracial” vision has not actually shifted the political landscape.

The pattern of rewarding loyalty over competence is also not new for PH. The Nurul Izzah adviser appointment, various political secretary scandals, and now Adam Adli all follow the same logic: political loyalty first, governance competence second.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You said you’d appoint ministers based on merit, not race. Then you made an appointment that triggered a racial firestorm, and instead of standing firm, your coalition partner UMNO rushed to reassure Malays. Pick a lane. Either you believe in multiracial meritocracy — and defend it without qualifiers — or admit that race still drives your Cabinet math. And while you’re at it, explain why a former student activist is overseeing religious affairs governance. Because “loyalty” is not a qualification.

Sources: SCMP | Straits Times | Malay Mail | FMT | US-ASEAN

item.title: Zafrul Appointed MIDA Chairman After Leaving MITI — The Revolving Door Never Stops Spinning
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MIDA Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the December 2025 cabinet reshuffle removed Tengku Zafrul from the MITI portfolio, he wasn’t sent to the backbench. Instead, Anwar appointed him as Chairman of MIDA (Malaysian Investment Development Authority) — the very agency that falls under MITI’s purview — and as Senior Political Advisor to the PM. The man who oversaw MIDA as minister now chairs it as a political appointee.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The revolving door between ministerial oversight and agency chairmanship is a textbook governance concern. Zafrul approved MIDA’s policies, budgets, and strategies as MITI Minister. Now he chairs the same agency he regulated. Investment decisions he influenced as minister — including the US trade deal’s implementation — will now be executed under his chairmanship. There is no cooling-off period, no separation, no firewall.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH campaigned on ending the revolving door between politics and GLCs. IDEAS documented 238 political appointments to GLCs and statutory bodies under Anwar — exceeding Mahathir 2.0’s rate. Zafrul’s MIDA appointment is the most brazen example: not even a pretence of distance between the ministerial role and the agency appointment. The same person, the same portfolio, different title. This is not governance reform; it’s musical chairs with name cards.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, ministers who lost portfolios were given GLC sinecures as compensation. PH criticised this practice relentlessly. Under Anwar, the practice continues with better PR — Zafrul’s appointment is framed as ‘continuity and expertise’ rather than what it is: a political reward and soft landing for a reshuffled ally.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You documented 238 political appointments. You campaigned against the GLC revolving door. Then you appointed your former MITI minister as chairman of MIDA — the agency MITI oversees. If this isn’t a political appointment, what is? Implement cooling-off periods. Create independent appointment committees. Or just admit that your patronage system works exactly like BN’s, with slightly better PR.

Sources: SCMP | FMT

item.title: Civil Service is 90% Bumiputera — Malays Hold 80%+ of Top Posts
item.description:
Said by: Eurasia Review Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to Eurasia Review analysis published in December 2025, Malaysia’s civil service of 1.3 to 1.6 million employees is approximately 90% Bumiputera. Malays hold 77.5% of all posts and over 80% of top management positions. Chinese Malaysians hold just 5.7% and Indian Malaysians 3.8% — in a country where Chinese make up 23% and Indians 7% of the population. The Straits Times reported the “Bumiputera-first policy debate” has taken on a “heady mix of health, education and politics.” When a UiTM professor suggested opening Bumiputera-only programmes to other races to address specialist shortages, the backlash was immediate and the idea was shut down.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A civil service that doesn’t reflect the country’s demographics cannot serve all citizens equally. Race-based hiring in public service perpetuates ethnic exclusion and undermines meritocracy — the very principle Anwar campaigned on. The civil service is simultaneously Malaysia’s largest employer and its most racially homogeneous institution. When 90% of government servants come from one ethnic group, the entire apparatus of state — from policy design to implementation to service delivery — is filtered through one cultural lens. Non-Bumiputera citizens interact with a government that barely represents them.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s “Malaysia Madani” brand promises a civilised, meritocratic, multiracial Malaysia. Yet the most visible arm of the Malaysian state — the civil service — remains 90% one race. Under PH, this is untouchable. Not because reform is impossible, but because 1.6 million civil servants are also 1.6 million votes — overwhelmingly Malay votes that Anwar cannot afford to lose ahead of GE16. The “reformist” government has done nothing — not a pilot programme, not a target, not even a public acknowledgment — to address the structural imbalance. Meritocracy applies to everyone except the government’s own workforce.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The NEP’s civil service quotas were introduced in the 1970s as affirmative action. Fifty years later, the “temporary” measures are permanent, the quotas are entrenched, and the racial composition has only become more homogeneous over time. Every PM — Mahathir, Abdullah, Najib, Muhyiddin, Anwar — has left this untouched. The UiTM incident is instructive: even suggesting partial reform triggered enough backlash to kill the idea instantly. The third rail of Malaysian politics isn’t just electrified — it’s surrounded by a moat.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your civil service is 90% one race in a multiracial country. Your “Madani” brand promises meritocracy. These two facts cannot coexist. Set diversity targets. Open recruitment. Create pathways for non-Bumiputera Malaysians to serve their own government. Or admit that “Malaysia Madani” is a slogan for export — a brand you sell to foreign investors while running a race-based state at home. 1.6 million civil servants, 90% Bumiputera. That’s not meritocracy. That’s a monoculture wearing a multiracial mask.

Sources: Eurasia Review | Straits Times

item.title: PM's Own Aide Shamsul Iskandar Charged with Bribery — Anti-Corruption Crusade Eats Itself
item.description:
Said by: Reuters / Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 4 December 2025, Anwar Ibrahim’s senior political secretary Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin was charged with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman. The charges allege Shamsul used his privileged position in the Prime Minister’s office to assist the businessman in obtaining mineral mining licences in Sabah, among other favours. Shamsul resigned just days before being charged, pleaded not guilty, and was granted bail. He called the charges an “attack” against him. Reuters reported the charges “significantly increased scrutiny on the premier’s pledge to crack down on corruption.” CNA reported Anwar now faces a “graft crisis” alongside “PKR party fractures” and “PH coalition jitters.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is not a minor official in a distant agency. This is the PM’s senior political secretary — someone with direct, daily access to the most powerful office in the country. The charges allege he was running a pay-for-play operation for mining licences from inside the PM’s office. If the allegations are true, the anti-corruption crusade’s own headquarters was a corruption venue. Reuters framing it as increasing “scrutiny on the premier’s pledge to crack down on corruption” is diplomatic understatement — this demolishes the pledge entirely.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: This is the same Anwar who built his entire political career on anti-corruption rhetoric. He spent decades as Malaysia’s moral compass on corruption. He went to prison (on charges he said were politically motivated). He built Reformasi as a movement against the abuse of power. And his closest aide was allegedly selling mining licences from the PM’s office. The irony is suffocating: the man who vowed to clean up Malaysia now has his own office as a crime scene. Shamsul calling the charges an “attack” echoes every corrupt Malaysian politician’s defence — and PH used to mock that defence when BN politicians used it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s history of aide and secretary corruption is extensive. Under Najib, aides and associates were implicated in 1MDB-related schemes. Under Muhyiddin, political secretaries were linked to procurement irregularities. The pattern: powerful aides use proximity to the PM to monetise access, knowing that their position provides both opportunity and protection. Anwar’s office is not different — it is the latest iteration. The Shamsul case is particularly damaging because it involves Sabah mining licences, connecting to the broader Sabah resource corruption ecosystem that PH promised to dismantle.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your closest aide was charged with bribery for selling access to mining licences from YOUR office. Not a distant state official. Not a minor functionary. Your political secretary. The person who sat outside your door. What did you know? When did you know it? Did Shamsul’s activities never come to your attention despite years of proximity? Either you knew and tolerated it, or you didn’t know — in which case your office management is dangerously negligent. Answer the question. The anti-corruption crusade doesn’t survive its own headquarters being a crime scene.

Sources: Reuters | CNA

item.title: Anwar Defends Support Letters in Parliament — Then Attacks Opposition for Doing the EXACT Same Thing
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim in Parliament / The Star

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-02

📌 What Happened

When confronted in parliament about his defence of ‘recommendation letters’ (surat sokongan) for government tenders, Anwar pivoted to attacking the opposition by revealing that Kelantan and Terengganu state officials had also issued similar letters. He said: ‘I must call out the hypocrisy — there was not a single word on the letters by the Kelantan and Terengganu exco asking for approval.’

🔥 Why It Matters

Instead of addressing whether support letters are appropriate (they aren’t — they undermine competitive procurement), Anwar’s defence was ’the other side does it too.’ This is the political equivalent of a child caught stealing saying ‘but Johnny stole too!’ It’s not a defence — it’s an admission that the practice is universal and nobody, including PH, intends to stop it.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The entire point of electing PH was that they were supposed to be DIFFERENT from BN/PN. ‘They do it too’ is not reform — it’s surrender. Anwar was specifically asked about his OWN government’s practice. Instead of committing to end it, he deflected to the opposition. The man who promised to clean up governance used his parliamentary time to argue that corruption is bipartisan — as if that makes it acceptable.

📜 Previous Cases

Mahathir defended similar patronage practices. Najib’s government was notorious for political interference in procurement. The whole point of Reformasi was to end this cycle. Instead, Anwar is perpetuating it and using ‘whataboutism’ as a governance strategy.

🎯 PH Callout

We didn’t elect you to be equally corrupt as the opposition. We elected you to be BETTER. ‘They do it too’ is not a reform agenda — it’s a confession that you’ve given up on reform. Stop pointing fingers at Kelantan and start cleaning up your own house. Or admit that PH’s anti-corruption platform was always just marketing.

Sources

  • The Star — Support Letters

item.title: Anwar's Three-Year Report Card: Steady Economics, Broken Promises, Collapsing Reform Credibility
item.description:
Said by: Multiple analysts and media

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As Anwar Ibrahim marked three years as Prime Minister in late 2025, multiple analysts and media outlets delivered a consistent verdict: steady economics, shattered reform credibility. GDP growth exceeded 5%, foreign investment hit record highs, and unemployment reached decade lows. But on reform — the actual reason PH was elected — the record is dismal: the Sedition Act was expanded with 90 arrests, UUCA remains intact, ICCPR is unratified, AG-PP separation was sent to committee after three years of delay, Zahid’s 47 corruption charges were dropped, MACC is under fire for politicisation, a journalist was arrested for asking a question, and Nurul Izzah was installed as PKR No. 2.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Three years is not “early days.” It is more than half a parliamentary term. If reform was coming, it would have started by now. Instead, what Malaysia has is a government that performs competent economic management while systematically abandoning the democratic promises that brought it to power. The trade-off is now explicit: you get GDP growth, but you lose civil liberties, institutional independence, and accountability. The question is no longer “will Anwar reform?” The question is: was reform ever the goal?

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s defence is always “we inherited a mess” and “give us time.” But they’ve had time — more time than PH 1.0 ever got. The Sedition Act wasn’t just left on the books — it was weaponised more aggressively than under Najib. UUCA wasn’t just kept — students who protested were ignored. The AG-PP separation wasn’t just delayed — it was tabled and then immediately sent to a committee to die quietly. Every single flagship reform promise has been either abandoned, delayed, or reversed. Economic competence is not a substitute for democratic accountability — it’s what authoritarian governments use to justify staying in power.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: PH 1.0 (2018-2020) had 22 months and accomplished partial reforms before collapsing via the Sheraton Move. PH 2.0 has had 36 months with a larger majority and accomplished fewer reforms. Mahathir’s first tenure showed how economic success can coexist with democratic regression for decades. The pattern is clear: Malaysian PMs use economic performance as a shield against reform demands, and it works — until it doesn’t.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You were elected on reform. You delivered economics. Those are not the same thing. BN also delivered economics — for decades — while gutting institutions. You are now doing the same thing with better PR. Repeal the Sedition Act. Abolish UUCA. Ratify ICCPR. Separate the AG and PP. Restore MACC independence. Or stop pretending you’re different from the people you replaced — because after three years, the only difference is the slogan.

Sources: Malaysiakini | FMT | The Edge

item.title: Zafrul: The Man Who Served Every PM — From Muhyiddin's Finance Minister to Anwar's Trade Minister to 'Senior Advisor'
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz has achieved what no other Malaysian politician has: serving in senior ministerial roles under three consecutive PMs from three different coalitions. He was Finance Minister under Muhyiddin (PN) and Ismail Sabri (BN-PN), then switched to PKR and became MITI Minister under Anwar (PH). After being reshuffled out in December 2025, he was immediately appointed Senior Political Advisor to the PM and MIDA Chairman. SCMP profiled him as ‘hot property’ — the man every PM wants.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: In a democracy, political leaders are supposed to represent ideological commitments and voter mandates. Zafrul represents neither — he represents technocratic survival. His ability to seamlessly serve Muhyiddin (who came to power via the Sheraton Move that PH calls a betrayal), then Ismail Sabri, then Anwar (who calls the Sheraton Move a coup) raises the question: what does Zafrul actually believe? The answer appears to be: whatever the current PM needs him to believe.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Zafrul is now testifying in Muhyiddin’s corruption trial as a witness — the same Muhyiddin who appointed him Finance Minister. He jumped from UMNO to PKR when the political winds shifted. PH welcomed him because he brings technocratic credibility and banking-sector connections. But Zafrul’s loyalty is to power, not to reform. When the next PM arrives — whoever that is — Zafrul will be ready to serve. That’s not public service; it’s political mercenary work with a good suit.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long tradition of political chameleons — figures who survive regime changes by being useful to everyone. But Zafrul has elevated it to an art form. His testimony in Muhyiddin’s trial while serving as Anwar’s advisor creates a conflict that would be disqualifying in most democracies.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You spent years condemning the Sheraton Move as a betrayal of democracy. Then you appointed the Sheraton Move’s Finance Minister as your trade minister and now your senior advisor. If Muhyiddin’s government was illegitimate, why is Muhyiddin’s FM your most trusted economic advisor? The answer: principles are for campaigns; power is for governance.

Sources: SCMP | Malay Mail

item.title: PH Wins 1 Out of 20 Seats in Sabah — Anwar Says Defeat Won't Stop Delivery
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 30 November 2025, Pakatan Harapan was demolished in the Sabah state election. The coalition won just 1 out of 20 seats it contested — down from 7 previously. That is a 95% loss rate. Sabah-based parties dominated, driven by anti-federal sentiment and demands for greater state autonomy — particularly after a High Court ruling supporting Sabah’s claim to 40% of federal revenue. Political analyst Lee Kuok Tiung said plainly: “The Sabah election outcome shows Anwar’s coalition lost badly.” Anwar’s response: the defeat “won’t stop delivery” of pledges to Sabah.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This was the first electoral test ahead of GE16 — and PH failed it catastrophically. Sabahans didn’t just reject PH — they rejected the entire idea of being governed by Peninsular-based parties. The results exposed a fundamental disconnect: Putrajaya’s priorities do not resonate in East Malaysia. Anwar appealed the High Court ruling on Sabah’s revenue share, directly antagonising the voters he needed. When you fight a state’s legal claim to its own resources and then ask the same state to vote for you, the outcome is predictable.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s response — “defeat won’t stop delivery” — is the political equivalent of losing a match 19-1 and insisting the training programme is working. If PH cannot win seats in Sabah, it cannot claim a mandate to govern Sabah. The promise to “deliver” to a state that overwhelmingly rejected you is not reassurance — it’s tone-deafness. Sabahans told PH exactly what they thought. PH’s response was to ignore the message and repeat the same platitudes. That’s not resilience; it’s denial.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: PH’s East Malaysia problem is not new. In GE15, PH won minimal seats in Sabah and Sarawak, relying on GPS and GRS for coalition numbers. The pattern is consistent: Peninsular parties treat East Malaysia as a vote bank rather than an equal partner. BN did this for decades. PH promised to be different — the Malaysia Agreement 1963 review, devolution of powers, revenue sharing. Three years later, Sabah got a court ruling in its favour and PH appealed it. The lesson from every Sabah election is the same: respect state autonomy or lose.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You won 1 out of 20 seats. One. Sabahans gave you the clearest possible message: you do not represent them. Instead of listening, you said “defeat won’t stop delivery.” Drop the appeal against Sabah’s revenue share ruling. Implement MA63 commitments. Devolve real power to East Malaysian states. Or keep winning 1 out of 20 and pretending it’s fine — because GE16 will deliver the same verdict if nothing changes.

Sources: Reuters | Foreign Policy | CNA | Lowy Institute

item.title: PM's Political Secretary Caught Issuing Support Letters for Hospital Contractors — Anwar Says a Reprimand Is Enough
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 18 November 2025, Anwar confirmed in Parliament that he had reprimanded his senior political secretary, Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, over support letters listing six contractors for a hospital project in Johor. Anwar described the letters as “vague.” On 25 November, Shamsul resigned after mounting media pressure and calls from Rafizi Ramli for a MACC investigation. A Sabah businessman then claimed he provided RM152,000 to Shamsul — money allegedly spent on home furniture, premium cigars, and tailored suits. On 4 December 2025, MACC charged Shamsul with corruption over RM176,829 in gratification related to the hospital project.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The PM’s office was literally issuing contractor support letters. This is the swamp Anwar promised to drain. Shamsul Iskandar was not a minor figure — he was a PKR veteran since the party’s founding, operating within the Prime Minister’s Department with direct influence over government procurement. When your own political secretary is funnelling contractor names to procurement officers and receiving RM152,000 in kickbacks spent on premium cigars and tailored suits, the anti-corruption brand is not just damaged — it is a punchline.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s first instinct was to minimise. “The letters were vague.” “A reprimand is sufficient.” The Edge reported that the support letters listed specific contractors by name — contradicting Anwar’s claim. Naming specific companies in a government support letter is not vague — it is a recommendation, and anyone in procurement knows exactly what it means. The timeline is damning: Shamsul issues letters → Anwar reprimands privately → media reports emerge → Anwar defends the reprimand in Parliament → businessman reveals RM152K payments → Shamsul resigns → MACC charges him. At every stage, Anwar was behind the curve — reacting to pressure rather than leading on accountability. If Rafizi had not publicly demanded action, would Anwar have done anything beyond a private reprimand? The evidence suggests no.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Support letters from political offices are one of Malaysia’s oldest patronage mechanisms. Under BN, political secretaries routinely monetised their proximity to power. Najib’s aide Jho Low operated with impunity for years before 1MDB collapsed. Under Muhyiddin, aide Ahmad Maslan faced corruption charges. The pattern is consistent across administrations: political aides operate in a grey zone of influence, and PMs look the other way until media pressure forces action. PH promised to end this culture. Instead, they replicated it — different party, same premium cigars.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your senior political secretary was charged with corruption for issuing support letters from your office. Your first response was a private reprimand. Your second response was to call the letters “vague.” Your third response — after RM152,000 in kickbacks surfaced — was to accept a resignation you should have demanded weeks earlier. Implement mandatory asset declarations for all political appointees. Ban support letters from ministerial offices. Publish procurement records for every project where support letters were issued. Or keep discovering corruption in your own office after the media does your job for you.

Sources: SCMP | CNA | Straits Times | The Edge

item.title: Selangor MB's Aide Accused of Unexplained Wealth — RM3M Bungalow, Luxury Trips, and Too Many GLC Posts
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, a newly created WhatsApp group named ‘MB Selangor’ — comprising state assembly members and leaders — triggered controversy by accusing a PKR leader close to Selangor Menteri Besar Amirudin Shari of unexplained wealth. The administrator questioned how the leader could afford a RM3 million bungalow, luxury overseas trips, and high-end hotel stays while holding numerous positions in state-linked agencies. The group asked: ‘Why is one person appointed to so many positions, as if there are no other qualified people in Selangor?’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The allegations dropped while Amirudin was tabling the Selangor Budget 2026 — timing that suggests deliberate political pressure, but that doesn’t make the questions invalid. When a single aide holds multiple GLC positions and displays a lifestyle inconsistent with public service income, the public deserves answers. The Selangor MB’s office dismissed the claims as ‘baseless’ and ‘politically motivated’ — the standard deflection used by every Malaysian politician since independence.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: ‘We will lodge a report with the authorities’ is not an answer to ‘how did your aide afford a RM3M bungalow.’ Filing a police report against accusers is intimidation, not accountability. If the wealth is legitimate, declare it. If the multiple GLC appointments are merit-based, publish the selection criteria. PH promised transparency — threatening legal action against people who ask questions about wealth is the opposite of transparency.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Unexplained wealth among political aides is a recurring pattern in Malaysian politics. Under BN, political secretaries and officers routinely accumulated wealth through proximity to power. PH criticised this relentlessly in opposition. Under Amirudin, the pattern continues — different coalition, same playbook.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If the allegations are false, prove it with asset declarations. If they’re true, investigate. But threatening reporters and WhatsApp administrators with legal action while refusing to answer the actual question — ‘how does a public servant afford this lifestyle?’ — is exactly what BN used to do. You called it corruption then. What do you call it now?

Sources: FocusM | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Parliament Reduced to Rubber Stamp — PM Whips Ensure Bills Pass Without Genuine Debate
item.description:
Said by: Opposition MPs / Parliamentary Reform Advocates

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite PH’s promise to strengthen parliamentary democracy, the Dewan Rakyat continues to function as a rubber stamp rather than a genuine deliberative body. Government bills pass with minimal amendment, committee work is perfunctory, and the government whip ensures coalition MPs vote in bloc. The PM term limit bill — one of PH’s signature reform promises — failed in Parliament because PH couldn’t even muster enough of its own MPs to support it. Fahmi Fadzil blamed “opposition confusion” for the failure, when 8 of PH’s own MPs didn’t show up to vote.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Parliament is supposed to be the check on executive power. When parliament is reduced to a venue for the PM’s speeches and pro-forma bill passage, democratic governance degrades into elected authoritarianism. Important legislation passes without meaningful debate. Bad policies go unchallenged. The PM term limit bill failure is the most damning example: PH had the numbers, had the mandate, and still couldn’t deliver on one of the simplest democratic reforms imaginable — because its own MPs couldn’t be bothered to attend.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH explicitly promised parliamentary reform: stronger select committees, an independent Speaker, greater allocation for opposition MPs, and meaningful debate on legislation. Some incremental changes were made, but the fundamental dynamics haven’t changed. The government controls the agenda, determines which bills are debated, and uses coalition arithmetic to ensure outcomes. Under BN, Najib’s government once passed 11 bills in a single day without proper debate. PH criticised this as democratic malpractice. Under PH, the pace is slightly slower but the outcomes are equally predetermined.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Parliamentary rubber-stamping is a Malaysian tradition that predates PH. Under Mahathir 1.0, parliament was a formality. Under Najib, the OSA, SOSMA, and NSC Act were rammed through with minimal debate. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) made some improvements — reviving select committees and increasing opposition funding — but these reforms were incomplete and have stalled under PH 2.0. The East Asia Forum documented how “Malaysia’s reform aspirations clashed with political realities in 2024,” noting that parliamentary independence remained a promise rather than a practice.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You control the Dewan Rakyat. You have a supermajority with BN’s support. You couldn’t even pass a PM term limit bill — your own signature reform. You had the numbers, you had the mandate, and you still couldn’t deliver. If you can’t limit your own power when you have the votes to do it, you were never going to limit your own power at all. Strengthen select committees with real investigative authority. Give the Speaker genuine independence. Allow free votes on reform bills. Or keep running a parliament that functions exactly like the one you inherited — just with different faces in the government benches.

Sources: East Asia Forum | Rosa Luxemburg

item.title: RTM Misidentifies THREE ASEAN Leaders on Live TV — Calls Singapore's PM 'Lee Hsien Loong' at Summit Malaysia Hosted
item.description:
Said by: RTM / Communications Ministry under Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 26 October 2025, during the live broadcast of the opening ceremony of the 47th ASEAN Summit — which Malaysia was hosting — the national broadcaster Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) misidentified three Southeast Asian leaders:

  • Singapore PM Lawrence Wong was called Lee Hsien Loong (his predecessor)
  • Thailand PM Anutin Charnvirakul was called Srettha Thavisin (his predecessor)
  • Indonesia President Prabowo Subianto was called Jokowi (his predecessor) RTM issued two separate apology statements. The broadcasting department said it “took the matter seriously” and that “appropriate action had been taken.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This was Malaysia’s moment on the world stage. The ASEAN Summit is the region’s premier diplomatic event, and Malaysia was the host. The entire region was watching. And Malaysia’s national broadcaster couldn’t get the names of three neighbouring heads of state right.

This isn’t a minor slip. These weren’t obscure officials — they were the leaders of Singapore, Thailand, and Indonesia, Malaysia’s closest neighbours. The fact that RTM’s commentators were working from outdated information suggests nobody at the broadcaster bothered to update their briefing materials. In the age of Google, this is inexcusable.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

RTM falls under the Communications Ministry, led by Fahmi Fadzil. This is the same ministry that talks about digital transformation, AI regulation, and building Malaysia’s media capabilities. They can’t even brief their commentators on who the current leaders of neighbouring countries are.

The apology — issued in two separate statements because they initially only acknowledged two of the three errors — was the cherry on top. You don’t get to embarrass Malaysia on live television at the summit Malaysia is hosting and then say “appropriate action has been taken” without telling anyone what that action was.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

RTM has been a running joke for decades. Underfunded, poorly managed, and treated as a government mouthpiece rather than a professional broadcaster. Under PH, there was talk of reforming state media. Instead, they got a Communications Minister who focuses on regulating TikTok for teenagers while RTM can’t name three world leaders correctly.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You hosted the ASEAN Summit. You wanted the world to see Malaysia as a serious, modern, competent nation. And your national broadcaster called three neighbouring leaders by their predecessors’ names on live TV. The entire region laughed at us. Mothership in Singapore ran it. CNA ran it. SCMP ran it. This is your communications infrastructure. This is your Madani brand. Fix it.

Sources: SCMP | Bernama | FMT | CNA

item.title: Najib's Lawyer: He May RETURN to Politics If Pardoned
item.description:
Said by: Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, Najib’s Lawyer

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-16

Najib’s lawyer openly floated his client’s return to politics if pardoned. UMNO demanded his pardon at their assembly. Anwar is stuck between needing UMNO’s support and maintaining anti-graft credentials.

item.title: RM200 Million SMUE Fund Allegedly Misappropriated — MACC Probes Selangor Senior Citizens' Scheme
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission launched an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of RM200 million from Selangor’s Skim Mesra Usia Emas (SMUE) — the state’s elderly welfare scheme. Multiple officials linked to the scheme were questioned. The SMUE fund was designed to provide financial assistance to Selangor’s senior citizens, making the alleged misappropriation particularly egregious — money meant for the elderly allegedly diverted elsewhere under PH’s watch.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: RM200 million is not a rounding error — it’s a massive sum allegedly stolen from a welfare programme for senior citizens. Selangor is PH’s crown jewel, the richest state, run continuously by PKR/PH since 2008. If RM200 million can go missing from an elderly welfare fund in a PH-controlled state, what does that say about PH’s claim to be the ‘clean governance’ coalition? The fund was supposed to help the most vulnerable. Instead, it may have helped insiders.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH has governed Selangor for nearly 18 years. This isn’t an inherited BN problem — this happened on PH’s watch, under PH’s appointees, in PH’s state. The party that built its brand on anti-corruption is facing a corruption probe in its safest state. Amirudin’s office has been conspicuously quiet about the investigation. No public statements demanding accountability. No suspension of officials. No independent audit announced. Silence.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This joins the sand mining probe, the MBI asset freeze, and the unexplained wealth allegations as part of a pattern. Selangor under PH isn’t a model of clean governance — it’s a state where MACC keeps finding things. At some point, one investigation is an outlier; four investigations is a pattern.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: RM200 million from a senior citizens’ fund. In your state. Under your watch. With your appointees. If BN did this, you’d hold a national rally. Since it’s your state, you hold a press conference about waste management. Demand a full public audit of SMUE. Suspend the officials. And tell the rakyat where the money went.

Sources: FMT | Facebook/FMT

item.title: Rafizi Warns: Farhash Allegations Undermine PM's Anti-Corruption Credentials
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-15

📌 What Happened

Rafizi publicly urged Anwar to confront allegations against Farhash Wafa, warning that Malaysia’s CPI flatlined at 50 points — the same score as 2015 during the peak 1MDB scandal. Said it’s ’extremely dangerous if the administration is seen as inconsistent in combating corruption.'

🔥 Why It Matters

When your own Economy Minister publicly warns that your anti-corruption credentials are collapsing, the problem is undeniable. Rafizi effectively told Anwar: your refusal to act against Farhash is measurably damaging the country’s corruption score. A CPI of 50 under Anwar is the same as under Najib during 1MDB — that comparison alone is devastating.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Rafizi’s warning was ignored; he was later sidelined from cabinet. The minister who told the truth was punished, while the aide accused of corruption was protected. This is not reform — it is the old playbook in new packaging.

🎯 PH Callout

Your most competent minister warned you publicly that protecting Farhash was destroying your anti-corruption credentials. Your response was to sideline the minister and protect the aide. In any reform government, that’s backwards. In yours, it’s standard operating procedure.

Sources

  • The Vibes — Rafizi on Farhash

item.title: Anwar Marks His Own Homework — PM Who Is Also Finance Minister Presents Budget and Claims It's Great
item.description:
Said by: Opposition / Budget Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-10

📌 What Happened

Budget 2026 was presented by Anwar Ibrahim — as Finance Minister — to parliament, where he sits as Prime Minister. He then defended his own budget in parliament, praised its achievements, and dismissed criticism. The person who wrote the exam is also the person who grades it.

🔥 Why It Matters

In any functional democracy, the finance function has oversight independent from the political leadership. When one person controls both the political agenda and the national wallet, there is no meaningful check on fiscal decisions. Budget allocations become political tools without independent scrutiny. Parliament’s role as a budget oversight body is undermined when the PM/FM controls both the executive and the fiscal agenda.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar criticised Najib SPECIFICALLY for holding both PM and FM portfolios, arguing it enabled 1MDB by removing oversight. Then Anwar took BOTH portfolios himself. When asked about this, his response was essentially ’trust me, I’m different.’ That’s not institutional reform — it’s personal exceptionalism. The same structural vulnerability that enabled 1MDB exists under Anwar.

📜 Previous Cases

Najib as PM+FM: 1MDB happened. Mahathir as PM: the Finance Minister role provided some separation. Under good governance principles, fiscal oversight requires institutional independence, not personal trust. Singapore separates the roles. The UK separates the roles. Most functional democracies separate the roles.

🎯 PH Callout

You were elected because the last PM who held both portfolios stole billions. Your solution to preventing the next 1MDB was… to replicate the exact same structural vulnerability. You’re marking your own homework, and unsurprisingly, you keep giving yourself A+. Appoint a separate Finance Minister or stop pretending institutional reform matters to you.

Sources

  • Reuters — Malaysia Budget & Living Costs

item.title: Tiong King Sing Serves Alcohol at Official Tourism Gala, Then Lies About Who Organised It
item.description:
Said by: Tiong King Sing, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Tiong King Sing came under fire in October 2025 after alcoholic beverages were served at a gala dinner during the Global Travel Meet — an event organised under the Tourism Malaysia banner. Video clips showed beer and wine being served to Muslim guests, prompting several to leave early.

Tiong initially denied responsibility, claiming the private sector organised the catering and that it was “not part of Tourism Malaysia’s official programme.” But former Health Minister Khairy Jamaluddin posted screenshots on Instagram showing Tourism Malaysia was listed as the organiser of the gala dinner, mocking Tiong with “Liar, liar pants on fire.”

Anwar eventually issued a “stern warning” to Tiong but did not sack him.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a minister lying on record about his own ministry’s event, getting caught with documentary evidence, and facing zero consequences beyond a “stern warning.” In a government that promised integrity and accountability, a minister who lies publicly and gets caught should face real consequences — not a pat on the wrist.

The incident also exposed the deep contradictions in Anwar’s coalition management. Tiong is from GPS (Gabungan Parti Sarawak), a critical coalition partner. Sacking him would risk losing Sarawak’s support. So accountability takes a back seat to coalition arithmetic.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Tiong’s defence was a masterclass in gaslighting. The Tourism Malaysia logo was on the event backdrop. Tourism Malaysia staff were present. But somehow it was the “private sector’s” fault. When Khairy produced the receipts, Tiong pivoted to expressing “deep regret” without actually apologising.

And Anwar’s response? A “stern warning.” The same Anwar who once promised that his ministers would be held to the highest standards. A minister who lies publicly about his own event gets a slap on the wrist. If this were a BN minister under Najib, PH would have demanded a resignation within 24 hours.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Tiong King Sing is the same minister who screamed “Do you know who I am?” at an airport in February 2025 — a pattern of entitlement and arrogance. He was also previously embroiled in controversies linked to alcohol consumption before this gala incident. The man has a track record of embarrassing the government and facing no real consequences.

Under BN, ministers who lied about public events were torn apart by the then-opposition. PH’s selective amnesia about accountability standards is a recurring theme.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

PH promised Kerajaan Madani — a civilised government built on integrity. A minister who lies about who organised an official event, gets caught red-handed, and faces only a “stern warning” is not integrity. It’s impunity. Either your standards apply to all your ministers — including coalition partners from Sarawak — or they mean nothing. Tiong should have been dropped from Cabinet. The fact that he wasn’t tells Malaysians everything they need to know about where coalition survival ranks versus accountability.

Sources: Malay Mail | CNA | SCMP | MalaysiaNow | The Star

item.title: Farhash Wafa Cleared by MACC Over Sabah Mining — Muda Slams 'Pleasing the PM'
item.description:
Said by: Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-09

📌 What Happened

MACC cleared Anwar’s former aide Farhash Wafa over the Sabah mining scandal, despite Bloomberg alleging Anwar ordered MACC not to investigate. Muda accused MACC chief Azam Baki of ’trying to survive by pleasing the PM.'

🔥 Why It Matters

When a PM’s own political aide is accused of corruption in a mining deal and the anti-corruption agency clears them without a credible public explanation, the agency’s independence is in question. Bloomberg’s allegation — that Anwar directly intervened to stop the investigation — is explosive. If true, it means the anti-corruption crusader is obstructing anti-corruption investigations. If false, the government has not provided evidence to refute it.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Either way, MACC’s credibility is shattered. An anti-graft agency that cannot investigate the PM’s inner circle is not an anti-graft agency — it is a political tool. The pattern is clear: allies get cleared, critics get investigated.

🎯 PH Callout

Your anti-corruption agency cleared your aide while Bloomberg reports you told them not to investigate. You haven’t denied the Bloomberg report with evidence. You haven’t explained why MACC’s clearing is credible. You’ve just moved on and hoped nobody would notice. We noticed.

Sources

  • MalaysiaNow — Farhash Cleared
  • Malaysiakini — MACC & Farhash

item.title: Anwar's Coalition Contradictions: Reformist PM, Corrupt DPM, and a Party That Enabled 1MDB
item.description:
Said by: Multiple analysts and commentators

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-01

The Impossible Coalition

Anwar Ibrahim governs Malaysia through a coalition that makes reform structurally impossible. The man who campaigned for decades against corruption now depends on the party that enabled the world’s largest kleptocracy.

The Cast of Characters

  • Anwar Ibrahim (PKR): Built his career on reformasi, anti-corruption, and clean governance.
  • Ahmad Zahid Hamidi (UMNO): Deputy PM. Faced 47 corruption charges. All dropped.
  • DAP: The coalition’s most consistent reform voice. Systematically sidelined in the December 2025 reshuffle.
  • UMNO: Najib’s party. Threatened to review coalition ties when a DAP MP celebrated Najib’s court loss.

The Structural Problem

As Fair Observer noted, Anwar’s coalition is fundamentally unstable because its members have opposing interests:

  • PH voters want reform, accountability, and institutional change.
  • UMNO wants protection for its leaders, patronage for its base, and Malay-first policies.
  • The harder Anwar pushes reform, the more he risks losing UMNO. The more he accommodates UMNO, the more he loses the reform base.

The Result

What you get is not reform. What you get is management. Coalition maintenance disguised as governance. Every major decision — the Zahid NFA, the cabinet reshuffle, the UUCA backtrack, the JAKIM guidelines U-turn — makes sense only through the lens of keeping the coalition together, not making Malaysia better.

*Source: *Fair Observer

item.title: Farhash Wafa Quits as Political Secretary — PM's War on Corruption Hits Own Office
item.description:
Said by: SCMP Report

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-01

Anwar’s top political secretary Farhash Wafa quit amid corruption allegations involving a Sabah mining deal. Bloomberg had previously alleged Anwar ordered MACC not to investigate. The anti-corruption crusade eating itself from the inside.

item.title: 18,000 March to Oust Anwar — 'Turun Anwar' Rally Fills Dataran Merdeka Over Broken Promises
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Public / Opposition Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 26 July 2025, an estimated 18,000 people filled Dataran Merdeka (Independence Square) in Kuala Lumpur, wearing black t-shirts and bandannas with the slogan “Turun Anwar” (Step Down Anwar). It was the first major anti-government protest since Anwar came to power in 2022.

Reuters reported: “Thousands of people rallied in the Malaysian capital to demand the resignation of Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, as public discontent grows over rising living costs and allegations of failing to deliver promised reforms.”

Former PM Mahathir Mohamad, aged 100, was the keynote speaker. He told the crowd: “He gave everyone RM100 but raised taxes instead. He doesn’t understand how badly the lower-income groups are suffering.”

Former PM Muhyiddin Yassin said people had been complaining about price hikes from the expanded sales and service tax.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

18,000 people don’t show up in the KL heat to protest for fun. This rally was a referendum on broken promises. The protest’s demands read like a scorecard of PH failures:

  • Cost of living not reduced
  • GE15 manifesto promises broken
  • Prices rising, salaries stagnant
  • Cronyism and nepotism worsening
  • Madani policy is “played on paper” only
  • Education and health neglected
  • The spirit of reformasi betrayed
  • Young people losing hope on housing and jobs Bloomberg’s headline said it all: “Anwar’s Broken Promises Spark Protests Demanding His Ouster.”

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

When Anwar was in opposition, he celebrated street protests as the voice of the rakyat. He marched with Bersih. He called for the right to peaceful assembly. Now that the streets are against him, the government’s response was to frame it as opposition-organised troublemaking.

The protest organisers listed 16 specific grievances. Not one was about race or religion. They were all about governance, economics, and accountability. This was a multiracial crowd united by disappointment in a government that promised everything and delivered excuses.

Al Jazeera noted it was “the first major protest since Anwar was propelled to power.” The significance is clear: the honeymoon is over.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysia has a proud tradition of street protest driving political change. Bersih rallies from 2007-2016 were instrumental in building the momentum that eventually toppled BN. Anwar was a beneficiary of that movement. Now the movement has turned on him. The irony is poetic.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

18,000 Malaysians stood in the sun to tell you they’re disappointed. Not angry at the opposition. Not manipulated by foreign agents. Disappointed in you. They marched because you promised reform and delivered status quo. You promised lower costs and delivered SST expansion. You promised clean governance and delivered Zahid’s DNAA. The streets that carried you to power are now asking you to leave. Are you listening?

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | Al Jazeera | Straits Times

item.title: Sabah Farhash Mining Licence — 70,000 Hectares, THREE Times Size of KL
item.description:
Said by: MalaysiaNow Investigation / Farhash Wafa

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-15

Farhash-linked company Bumi Suria applied for coal exploration licence covering 70,000 hectares of Sabah forest reserve — three times the size of KL. The area borders Kalimantan. MACC later cleared him.

item.title: MACC Seeks to Seize RM3 Billion in Daim Family Assets — Critics Ask If It's Justice or Vendetta
item.description:
Said by: MACC (Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-11

Seizing RM3 Billion From a Dead Man’s Family

In July 2025, MACC announced it was seeking to seize and forfeit more than RM3 billion in assets across 11 countries linked to the late Daim Zainuddin, his family, and associates. Daim had died eight months earlier, maintaining his innocence.

The Facts

Fact: Daim served twice as Finance Minister and was credited with helping Malaysia recover from the 1997 Asian Financial Crisis.

Fact: He was charged in January 2024 for failing to declare assets, following Pandora Papers revelations. The charge was dropped after his death in November 2024.

Fact: Despite his death, MACC continued investigations and issued fresh forfeiture notices in December 2025.

Fact: Daim had denied all wrongdoing, saying the investigations were “politically motivated.”

Fact: Bloomberg asked directly: “Could Malaysia seize $700 million from a dead tycoon?”

Why It Matters

  • If Daim’s wealth was illegally obtained, forfeiture is justified. But pursuing a dead man’s family while Zahid’s 47 charges were dropped raises uncomfortable questions about selective justice.
  • MACC’s credibility depends on consistency. Going after Daim’s billions while defending Azam Baki’s share scandal looks like political targeting. *Sources: Bloomberg · Bloomberg · *Malay Mail

item.title: Lim Guan Eng Corruption Trial — MACC Officer Testifies About Money Given to Former Penang CM
item.description:
Said by: Lim Guan Eng, former Penang CM / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 10 July 2025, a MACC investigating officer told the Sessions Court that during a fraud investigation in December 2019, Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli — then director of Consortium Zenith Construction (CZCSB) — revealed he had given money to both G. Gnanaraja and Lim Guan Eng in connection with the Penang undersea tunnel project. MACC Senior Superintendent Ng Heng Jun confirmed he had informed his superiors about Zarul’s statement. The tunnel project, worth RM6.3 billion, has been described by MACC witnesses as ‘akin to a land scam.’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Lim Guan Eng was DAP’s most prominent leader and Penang’s Chief Minister for 10 years. He’s the face of DAP’s governance model. If the tunnel corruption allegations are proven, it doesn’t just implicate one politician — it implicates DAP’s entire Penang legacy. A RM6.3 billion project that MACC witnesses describe as a ’land scam’ would be the biggest corruption case linked to a DAP leader in the party’s history.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP has positioned itself as the anti-corruption party for decades. Its entire brand is clean governance and meritocracy. The Penang tunnel trial threatens to demolish that brand. And DAP’s response? Silence from the national leadership. No public demand for full accountability. No independent review of Penang’s procurement processes under Guan Eng. The party that demands accountability from everyone else cannot bring itself to demand it from its own.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The tunnel project was approved during Guan Eng’s tenure, using a ‘request for proposal’ mechanism rather than open tender — a procurement method that critics say favours connected companies. The same procurement opacity was criticised in the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). Under DAP, Penang’s mega-projects consistently avoided open tender processes.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your former Penang CM is on trial for corruption related to a RM6.3 billion project that MACC calls a ’land scam.’ Your current Penang CM says the tunnel project is ‘still active.’ Your party has said nothing meaningful about accountability. Either demand full transparency from your own leaders or stop pretending DAP is the anti-corruption party. You can’t have it both ways.

Sources: Malay Mail

item.title: Civil Service Reform: Three Years of Talk, Zero Structural Change — 1.6 Million Bureaucrats Untouched
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS / Public Policy Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 What Happened

Anwar Ibrahim entered office promising sweeping civil service reform — modernisation, meritocracy, right-sizing a bloated bureaucracy that consumes a massive share of the national budget. Three years later, the civil service remains essentially unchanged: 1.3-1.6 million employees (excluding police and military), 90% Bumiputera, consuming approximately 40% of total government operating expenditure in salaries and pensions.

🔥 Why It Matters

IDEAS think tank found that the Anwar government actually increased political appointments to GLCs and statutory bodies, averaging 47.5 per year — exceeding even Mahathir 2.0’s rate of 43 per year. The bloated bureaucracy means Malaysia spends more on civil servant wages than on development spending. Pension obligations continue to balloon with no structural reform.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Proposals for performance-based assessments, right-sizing, and merit-based promotions have gone nowhere because the civil service vote is politically crucial — no government wants to antagonise 1.6 million voters and their families. Reform was always a lie; the civil service is PH’s vote bank just as it was BN’s.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised meritocracy. You delivered more political appointments than Mahathir. You promised right-sizing. The bureaucracy grew. You promised modernisation. The same inefficiencies persist. At some point, ‘reform’ stops being a promise and becomes a punchline. You passed that point two years ago.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: GLCs as Political Dumping Ground — 47.5 Appointees Per Year, Even MORE Than Mahathir 2.0
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS Think Tank Research

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 What Happened

Research by IDEAS think tank found that the Anwar government appointed an average of 47.5 political appointees per year to Government-Linked Companies (GLCs) and statutory bodies — exceeding even Mahathir 2.0’s rate of 43 per year and far surpassing the stated goal of merit-based appointments. A total of 238 political appointments were documented across Anwar’s first years.

🔥 Why It Matters

GLCs control a massive share of the Malaysian economy — Petronas, Khazanah, TNB, Telekom, MAHB, and dozens more. When board positions are filled based on political loyalty rather than competence, the consequences are enormous: poor strategic decisions, corruption, and mismanagement. Every political appointee who lacks the skills for their role costs the economy in efficiency, innovation, and shareholder value.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH ran on an EXPLICIT platform of ending political patronage in GLCs. Anwar himself criticised BN’s practice. He promised merit-based appointments and performance accountability. Instead, he’s appointing political allies at a FASTER rate than his predecessors. The reformist who promised to end patronage has industrialised it.

📜 Previous Cases

Under Najib, GLC appointments were a cornerstone of UMNO patronage. The 1MDB scandal itself was partly enabled by politically-appointed boards that failed in oversight. Under Mahathir 2.0, 43/year. Under Anwar, 47.5/year. Each government promises reform; each government entrenches patronage deeper.

🎯 PH Callout

47.5 political appointees per year. MORE than Mahathir’s government. You didn’t reform GLC governance — you accelerated the patronage machine. Every unqualified political appointee on a GLC board represents a qualified professional passed over. This is corruption with extra steps.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies
  • Rosa Luxemburg — Malaysia 2024

item.title: 2025 Judicial Appointment Controversy — Anwar's Pick for Chief Justice Sparks Outrage
item.description:
Said by: Legal Community / Wikipedia Documentation

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-01

Judicial appointment controversy erupted after Chief Justice Tengku Maimun retired. Anwar’s replacement picks raised concerns about judicial independence and executive overreach. The man who was once the judiciary’s most famous victim now undermines its independence.

item.title: Syed Saddiq Acquitted After '5 Years of Hell' — Was It Political Prosecution?
item.description:
Said by: Court of Appeal / Prosecution

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-25

📌 What Happened

Court of Appeal unanimously acquitted Syed Saddiq of all 4 charges, admonishing the High Court for not throwing out the case earlier. He had been sentenced to 7 years jail and caning by the lower court. The prosecution then appealed to the Federal Court, which gave a rare ‘cooling-off period.’ SCMP called it ‘5 years of hell.’

🔥 Why It Matters

A young opposition politician was put through half a decade of legal proceedings that the Court of Appeal said should have been dismissed far earlier. Whether or not it was politically motivated, the system failed him. The original conviction was so weak that the appellate court was visibly frustrated.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

If the judiciary can do this to a sitting MP, it can do it to anyone. The case raises questions about prosecutorial discretion under successive AGs — and why the government appealed even after the acquittal. Selective prosecution remains Malaysia’s most potent political weapon.

🎯 PH Callout

You claim to champion judicial independence and due process. But when an opposition MP is acquitted after five years of hell — and your prosecution service still appeals — you’re not defending justice, you’re prolonging persecution. Drop the appeal or explain why the Court of Appeal’s unanimous verdict isn’t good enough.

Sources

  • SCMP — Syed Saddiq Acquitted
  • FMT — Federal Court Cooling-Off

item.title: CSIS Declares 'The Death of Reformasi' — Anwar's Own Movement Pronounced Dead by Washington Think Tank
item.description:
Said by: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of Washington’s most influential policy think tanks, published a devastating analysis titled ‘The Death of Reformasi: Anwar Ibrahim, UMNO, and the Betrayal of a Movement.’ The piece argues that Anwar’s political identity has been ‘increasingly defined by the compromises he has made to secure his position within the government.’

🔥 Why It Matters

Reformasi wasn’t just a political movement — it was a generational promise. Millions of Malaysians, from university students to working-class families, invested decades of hope, protest, and sacrifice in the belief that Anwar Ibrahim represented a fundamentally different kind of politics. CSIS declaring it dead isn’t opposition rhetoric — it’s the verdict of the international policy establishment.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar spent 11 years in prison, endured two sodomy trials, survived political exile, and built an entire movement around one word: reform. He asked Malaysians to trust him. They did. And what did they get? A coalition with UMNO, the very party that imprisoned him. Zahid Hamidi, who was UMNO’s attack dog, is now his deputy. The man who fought the system IS the system.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, PH won on a reform mandate but collapsed within 22 months due to internal betrayal (Sheraton Move). Anwar was supposed to be the corrective — the leader who would finally deliver. Instead, he formed an even more compromised coalition than PH 1.0.

🎯 PH Callout

You built a movement on the promise of change. You asked people to march, to risk arrest, to believe. And then you partnered with the exact people they marched against. That’s not pragmatism — that’s the ultimate betrayal. Reformasi isn’t dead because it failed. It’s dead because you killed it.

Sources

  • CSIS — The Death of Reformasi

item.title: Nik Nazmi Resigns as Environment Minister After Losing PKR Party Election — Exposes PH's Feudal Politics
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On May 28, 2025, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad tendered his resignation from Cabinet, effective July 4. His resignation came hours after Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli also resigned — both following their defeats in PKR’s internal party elections five days earlier.

Nik Nazmi lost his PKR vice-president position and said he could no longer serve as minister without a full party mandate. “Being a minister can trap you in a ‘bubble’ — with privileges, security escorts, and protocols at every event,” he said. “I believe that becoming too attached to such a lifestyle can be dangerous.”

Anwar publicly asked Nik Nazmi to reconsider. Nik Nazmi refused, saying his decision was firm and he would focus on serving his Setiawangsa constituents.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Two Cabinet ministers resigning within hours of each other over internal party politics is not normal governance. It exposes the fact that PH ministerial appointments are fundamentally driven by party faction dynamics, not competence or national interest.

The PKR party election that triggered these resignations was itself marred by allegations of vote-rigging and nepotism — Anwar’s daughter Nurul Izzah defeated Rafizi for deputy president. When the PM’s daughter wins an internal contest and the losers are forced out of Cabinet, that’s not democratic renewal. That’s feudal politics dressed up in reform rhetoric.

The simultaneous departure of the Economy Minister and Environment Minister created months of policy uncertainty in two critical portfolios, with vacancies unfilled until December 2025.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Nik Nazmi deserves some credit for his principled stance — he’s one of the few Malaysian politicians who voluntarily gave up power. But the system that produced this outcome is rotten.

PKR’s party elections are supposed to be internal democratic exercises. Instead, they became proxy wars for the Anwar family’s consolidation of power. Nurul Izzah’s victory over Rafizi wasn’t just a party contest — it was the PM’s daughter defeating his most capable economic technocrat, leading to months of policy vacuum in the Economy Ministry.

And Anwar’s response? He begged Nik Nazmi to stay — not because of policy continuity, but because two ministerial resignations looked bad. The PM couldn’t even control the fallout from his own party’s elections. This is the “reformist” party that’s supposed to be different from UMNO’s feudal politics.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

UMNO’s history is littered with internal power struggles that spilled into governance — factional fights between Mahathir and Ku Li, between Mahathir and Anwar, between Najib and Muhyiddin. PKR was supposed to be different. It was founded on reformasi principles. Instead, it’s replicated the exact same patronage-driven internal politics, complete with a dynastic succession battle.

The PKR 2025 election also echoed the 2018 party polls, which were marred by similar allegations of irregularities. The party has never managed a clean internal election in its entire history.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You built PKR on the promise of democratic reform. Then your party election was marred by vote-rigging allegations, your daughter defeated your most prominent economic reformer, and two ministers resigned in protest. The result? Months of policy vacuum in the Economy and Environment ministries. This isn’t reform — it’s UMNO with better branding. If PKR can’t even run a clean internal election, why should Malaysians trust you to reform the country?

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | Malay Mail | ISEAS

item.title: Rafizi Ramli Resigns as Economy Minister After PKR Humiliation — The Reformist Who Got Purged
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 28 May 2025, Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli submitted his resignation from Cabinet, effective 17 June. His ally, Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, also announced his exit, effective 4 July.

Rafizi said: “I entered politics to cultivate a new political culture grounded in accountability and the people’s mandate. My recent defeat in the PKR elections signifies that I no longer possess the party’s mandate.”
